TRACH CHANGE NOTE — OUTPATIENT IP CLINIC
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD   PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 57F

Reason: Routine quarterly tracheostomy tube exchange; non-healing granulation tissue at stoma; complaint of blood-tinged secretions x2 days.
Current tube: Bivona 7.0 TTS (tight-to-shaft) cuffed, in place 3 months.
Procedure: Cuff deflated; tube removed (slight resistance, resolved with rotation). 10 Fr red rubber catheter guide placed through stoma into trachea. New Bivona 7.0 TTS tube advanced over guide; inner cannula locked. Position confirmed clinically (breath sounds, SpO2 stable, phonation with finger occlusion).
Granulation tissue at superior stoma margin: 2 × 3 mm; treated with silver nitrate stick x2 applications. Hemostasis achieved.
Complications: None. EBL: <1 mL.
Plan: Next exchange in 3 months. Otolaryngology referral placed for persistent stomal granulation; consider electrocautery at next IP visit if not resolved.

PLEURAL PROCEDURE CHECKLIST — ULTRASOUND-GUIDED THORACENTESIS
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE: 77F

INDICATION: Dyspnea; right-sided transudative effusion, CHF exacerbation (J81.0)

PRE-PROCEDURE CHECKLIST:
  ☑ Consent obtained (written)
  ☑ Time-out completed
  ☑ Coagulation reviewed: INR 1.1, Plt 188k — acceptable
  ☑ Ultrasound survey completed
  ☐ Sedation required — NOT required; local anesthetic only
  ☑ Portable CXR baseline reviewed

ULTRASOUND FINDINGS:
  Probe: Sonosite S-series, phased array 1–5 MHz
  Right side: Large anechoic effusion; estimated depth 5.2 cm at marked site; lung sliding absent in right base; no internal echogenicity (transudate morphology); diaphragm well-visualized
  Left side: Small trace effusion; not tapped

ENTRY POINT: 0‌ Right posterior, 7th ICS, posterior axillary line 1‌ [unicode artifact retained for stress testing]
Needle: 17-gauge Turkel safety thoracentesis kit

PROCEDURE:
Local anesthetic 1% lidocaine 8 mL infiltrated. Thoracentesis needle advanced under direct real-time ultrasound guidance; pleural fluid confirmed by aspiration. Catheter advanced; 1,050 mL amber clear fluid drained without difficulty. Lung sliding returned at RLL on ultrasound at procedure conclusion. No pneumothorax on post-procedure assessment. Patient reported mild pleuritic discomfort during drainage; drainage paused briefly at 800 mL — resumed without complication.

POST-PROCEDURE CHECKLIST:
  ☑ Post-procedure US: no pneumothorax, re-expansion confirmed
  ☑ Specimens sent to lab
  ☑ Patient observed 30 min post-procedure
  ☑ Discharge instructions provided

SPECIMENS: Right pleural fluid 1,050 mL — LDH, protein, glucose, cytology, culture

COMPLICATIONS: None. Re-expansion pulmonary edema prophylaxis: drainage capped at 1,500 mL; limit not reached.

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, malformed_codes

MONTGOMERY T-TUBE INSERTION — POST-INTUBATION TRACHEAL STENOSIS
OPERATIVE REPORT
DATE: [REDACTED]   ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP
ENT CO-SURGEON: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 38M

PLAN (documented at top per department template):
1. IP clinic follow-up 2 weeks.
2. T-tube bronchoscopy check (flexible via sidearm) at 4 weeks.
3. SLP for Passy-Muir trial (T-tube capped state allows voicing).
4. Surgical candidacy for tracheal resection reassessment at 12 months.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Post-intubation tracheal stenosis, subglottic, grade III (J95.5)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; T-tube in satisfactory position

PROCEDURE CODES:
  31631-22     — Rigid bronchoscopy, complex (increased complexity for T-tube placement with laser)
  31641        — Therapeutic bronchoscopy (laser ablation)
  [T-tube code: unlisted — 31899; billing: confirm 31899:22 modifier vs 31631-22 per payer; coding addendum to follow]

ANESTHESIA: GA; TIVA; spontaneous ventilation.

PROCEDURE: Rigid bronchoscopy (8.5 → 10.5 → 12.5 mm scopes sequential) confirmed subglottic grade III stenosis at 1.2 cm below vocal cords; fibrous mature web; 4 mm residual lumen. Nd:YAG radial incisions (4 positions). Balloon dilation: 10 → 12 → 14 mm. Post-dilation lumen 13 mm. Stoma created at ring 2–3 under transillumination. T-tube (13 × 60 × 30 mm) introduced; upper limb seated above cords; sidearm exteriorized. Flexible scope confirmed position. Voice test: clear phonation with sidearm plugged.

SPECIMENS: None.

COMPLICATIONS: Grade I mucosal lacerations. Transient SpO2 dip to 88% during proximal limb placement — spontaneous ventilation maintained; FiO2 increased; recovered within 60 seconds. EBL: 15 mL.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: inconsistent_formatting, noisy_units

PROCEDURE NOTE: ENDOBRONCHIAL ULTRASOUND AND NAVIGATIONAL BRONCHOSCOPY — COMBINED CASE
DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
ASSISTANT: [NAME:REDACTED], PA-C
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   AGE/SEX: 66-year-old female   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]

INDICATION: 1.9 cm LLL nodule; PET positive (SUVmax 4.3); enlarged station 7 node (1.8 cm); biopsy both targets.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: LLL pulmonary nodule and mediastinal adenopathy, indeterminate (R91.1, R59.9)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Pending

PROCEDURE LIST
  • EBUS-TBNA, stations 7, 4L (CPT 31652)
  • Navigational bronchoscopy, peripheral — computer-assisted (CPT 31627)
  • Radial EBUS (CPT 31625 — unlisted add-on, or bronchoscopy w/ biopsy 31628)
  • TBBX x4 via navigational catheter

ANESTHESIA: General; LMA; TIVA propofol infusion; fentanyl bolus 100 mcg, paralytic for LMA placement.

INSTRUMENTS: Olympus BF-UC180F (EBUS); Medtronic superdimension 7th-gen ENB system; radial EBUS probe (2.0 mm, 20 MHz); Olympus BF-P290 used for peripheral navigation.

PROCEDURE NARRATIVE:

EBUS PHASE:
Linear EBUS scope introduced. Mediastinal survey:
  - Station 4L: 1.2 cm, hypoechoic — 3 passes; ROSE pending
  - Station 7: 1.8 cm, heterogeneous with central necrosis pattern — 4 passes; ROSE: malignant (adenocarcinoma morphology)
  - Station 4R: surveyed, <0.5 cm, not sampled

NAVIGATIONAL PHASE (scope exchanged to thin bronchoscope + ENB):
ENB route planned to LLL superior segment (B6). Extended working catheter advanced; fluoroscopic landmark verification at LLL target. Shape-confirmed position achieved per system readout. Radial EBUS: partial (eccentric) ring around 8 mm halo — consistent with lesion at/near probe. Four transbronchial biopsies obtained (1.8 mm forceps, fluoroscopy-guided). BAL 60 mL instilled (30 mL returned) from LLL B6.

Medication log:
  Midaz [sic — should be propofol TIVA per above] 2mg IV pre-induction
  Fentanyl 10 0 mcg [noisy unit — 100 mcg intended]
  Propofol infusion 80–120 mcg/kg/min

SPECIMENS:
Specimen Label | Site | Technique | Container
4L node (3p) | Station 4L | EBUS-TBNA | Cytology + cell block
7 node (4p) | Station 7 | EBUS-TBNA | Cytology + cell block + molecular hold
LLL nod Bx | LLL B6 | TBBX x4 | Formalin
LLL BAL | LLL B6 | BAL 60mL | Cytology + culture

COMPLICATIONS: None. EBL: <2 mL. No pneumothorax; post-procedure CXR ordered.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
Station 7 ROSE positive for malignancy (adenocarcinoma); this is sufficient for staging if confirmed on formal pathology.
LLL biopsy results pending; if discordant with station 7, will repeat vs proceed to CT-guided biopsy.
Molecular reflex testing ordered per lung cancer protocol (PD-L1, NGS panel).
Oncology referral placed.

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, ambiguous_laterality, malformed_codes

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY
PROCEDURE NOTE — NAVIGATIONAL BRONCHOSCOPY
FACILITY: [NAME:REDACTED] Cancer Center
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED]
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 72M

PLAN (recorded at start of note per department protocol):
1. Results to lung cancer MDC.
2. Molecular reflex if malignant confirmed.
3. Robotic platform procedure — if non-diagnostic, CT-guided FNA as next step.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Left lower lobe 2.4 cm mass, PET-avid (SUVmax 9.1) (R91.1 / C34.12 — provisional)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Pending — LLL mass, biopsied

PROCEDURES (billing codes):
  31627     Bronchoscopy, navigational
  31625     Bronchoscopy w/ biopsy (additional lobe)
  31623-59  Brushings [modifier 59 — distinct procedural service; billing note: confirm 31623:59 vs 31623-59 format per payer]
  99100     Anesthesia — patient age >70

ANESTHESIA: GA; TIVA (propofol/remifentanil); size 7.0 ETT via direct laryngoscopy

CLINICAL CONTEXT: 72-year-old male, 45 pack-year smoker, COPD GOLD 3. CT chest: 2.4 cm spiculated LLL mass; PET SUVmax 9.1; no hilar or mediastinal adenopathy. Pulmonary function: FEV1 52% predicted. Referred for bronchoscopic biopsy as surgical risk prohibitive.

INSTRUMENTS: Ion Robotic Bronchoscopy System; 3.5 mm robotic catheter; 1.1 mm cryoprobe (Erbe); radial EBUS probe 20 MHz

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
ETT placed. Robotic bronchoscope introduced via ETT. Survey of bilateral airways: copious thick secretions cleared bilaterally (consistent with COPD). Bilateral lower lobe bronchi patent to subsegmental level. Robotic navigation plan loaded.

Target: LLL (left lower lobe) — superior segment (B6), posterior inferior nodule coordinates registered from pre-op CT. Catheter navigated; shape-sensing confirmed position within 5 mm of target. Radial EBUS performed — halo sign identified (eccentric partial ring), lesion adjacent.

Initial biopsy: bronchoscopic forceps biopsy x2 (marginal tissue, small fragments). Cryoprobe introduced: 3 freeze-thaw cycles (1.5 seconds each); substantial tissue core retrieved each time x3. Brushings x3. BAL LLL B6 (50 mL, 28 mL return).

Note: Fluoroscopic confirmation used at LLL target — probe appeared in left [confirmed LLL, not right — ambiguity noted at real-time and corrected] lower lobe B6 on AP and lateral views.

SPECIMENS:
  A: LLL cryobiopsies x3 (cryoprobe, 1.5 sec cycles) — formalin
  B: LLL forceps biopsy x2 — formalin
  C: LLL brushings x3 — ThinPrep
  D: LLL BAL (50 mL) — cytology, AFB, fungal Cx

COMPLICATIONS: Mild hemorrhage post-cryobiopsy; resolved with cold saline irrigation and 1:20,000 epi 2 mL topical. No pneumothorax. EBL: ~5 mL.

NOTE: note_029

NOTE TEXT:

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: extreme_length, inconsistent_formatting, copy_paste_artifacts

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY — OPERATIVE REPORT
PERCUTANEOUS DILATIONAL TRACHEOSTOMY (MODIFIED GRIGGS TECHNIQUE)
INSTITUTION: [NAME:REDACTED] Medical Center
DATE: [REDACTED]   ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD   RESIDENT: [NAME:REDACTED], MD (PGY-4, surgery)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 48M   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSES:
  1. Anoxic brain injury following out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (G93.1); ICU day 11
  2. Ventilator-dependent respiratory failure (J96.00)
  3. Failed percutaneous tracheostomy attempt x1 (two days prior, separate team — abandoned due to difficult anatomy; documented separately)

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSES:
  Same; Portex 8.0 cuffed low-profile trach placed; bronchoscopic confirmation satisfactory

CPT: 31600; 31622

ANESTHESIA: Propofol infusion 100–140 mcg/kg/min; fentanyl 150 mcg IV in divided doses; vecuronium 10 mg IV for paralysis. No anesthesiologist; attending performed procedural sedation per ICU protocol. Certified CRNA available in adjacent bay.

CLINICAL BACKGROUND:
48-year-old male 48-year-old [sic] admitted after OHCA, ROSC achieved after 18 min CPR. Cooled to 33°C for 24h (TTM protocol complete). Neurological assessment: no purposeful movement; MRI brain: diffuse cortical/subcortical anoxic injury; multidisciplinary goals-of-care conference with family ongoing; decision to proceed with tracheostomy for ongoing airway management pending prognosis clarification. Prior PDT attempt (day 9): abandoned — neck circumference 48 cm, high-riding innominate artery identified on ultrasound, team elected to abort.

ULTRASOUND PLANNING SESSION (performed morning of procedure):
Attending performed dedicated cervical/tracheal ultrasound (pre-procedure):
  - High-frequency linear probe (7–13 MHz)
  - Tracheal rings identified: ring 2–3 interspace marked (1.8 cm below cricoid)
  - Vasculature mapping: thyroid gland midline, no isthmus at ring 2–3; innominate artery loop identified on prior attempt (day 9) now assessed — artery lies 2.2 cm below ring 3 (safe margin); paratracheal vessels mapped; no midline vessel at entry point
  - Skin-to-trachea depth: 4.1 cm (deep — large habitus)
  - Entry site confirmed with confidence; no contraindication to proceed at ring 2–3

EQUIPMENT MODIFICATION (per difficult anatomy plan):
  - Griggs guidewire dilating forceps (GWDF) technique selected (over Blue Rhino) for better tactile control in deep-tissue anatomy
  - Portex PDT kit with GWDF
  - Extended-length Shiley 8.0 cuffed XLT (length 105 mm vs standard 80 mm) selected given 4.1 cm tissue depth
  - Olympus BF-P290 bronchoscope; extra-length working channel for depth

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
Patient supine, neck extended on folded blanket (shoulder roll). Neck circumference 48 cm — significant subcutaneous tissue present. Pre-procedure SpO2 97% on FiO2 0.70, PEEP 8. Skin prepared; sterile drape. ETT position: 7.5 ETT at 22 cm; withdrawn to 17 cm under bronchoscopic guidance; carina 9 cm distal.

Under direct bronchoscopic illumination: transillumination of anterior tracheal wall confirmed at ring 2–3 level (faint but adequate given depth of tissue). Real-time ultrasound probe manned by assisting resident — confirmed needle in midline at ring 2–3 on ultrasound B-mode simultaneously.

14-gauge introducer needle advanced under combined US + bronchoscopic guidance. Needle tip confirmed in tracheal lumen at ring 2–3, midline. J-wire advanced without resistance; wire in tracheal lumen confirmed. Small longitudinal fascial dilator (12 Fr). Griggs GWDF forceps loaded on wire; forceps advanced closed, then opened under bronchoscopic visualization — tracheal lumen widened step-wise to accommodate tube. One additional GWDF pass required (total 2 passes; adequate dilation confirmed on bronchoscopy). Extended Portex 8.0 XLT tube (length 105 mm) loaded on obturator, advanced over wire, seated; obturator removed; cuff inflated 24 mmHg (manometer).

Ventilator circuit connected. ETCO2 present. Bilateral breath sounds on auscultation. Bronchoscopic check through trach: tube tip 5 cm above carina (satisfactory for XLT tube), midline, posterior wall intact, no hemorrhage, no false passage.

Site secured: 2-0 silk suture x2 to flange; Velcro holder. Trach dressing applied.

SPECIMENS: None.

COMPLICATIONS: None. SpO2 nadir 92% at dilation — brief; FiO2 increased to 1.0 x2 minutes; recovered to 97%. EBL: approximately 8 mL. No pneumothorax. No posterior wall injury. No arterial injury.

POST-PROCEDURE ORDERS:
1. CXR portable — confirm trach position.
2. FiO2 wean per protocol after post-procedure CXR reviewed.
3. Trach site dressing: daily; inner cannula q8h cleaning.
4. Cuff pressure q8h; target 20–25 mmHg.
5. Neurology and palliative care co-management ongoing — trach does not constitute a terminal or curative decision; family meeting scheduled.
6. PMV assessment deferred (GCS 3T).

NOTE: note_017

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: conflicting_details, copy_paste_artifacts

BRONCHOSCOPY NOTE — THERAPEUTIC
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 44F
DATE: [REDACTED]   ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Bronchopleural fistula post-lobectomy (J86.0); persistent air leak, right chest tube day 14

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; endobronchial valve placement performed

PROCEDURES: Flexible bronchoscopy with endobronchial valve placement, Spiration IBV (CPT 31647)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation. Midazolam 2 mg + fentanyl 75 mcg IV [Note: anesthesia intake form lists "general anesthesia" — review of record confirms moderate sedation used in IP suite; intake form error as with prior cases].

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
44-year-old female s/p right upper lobectomy for T1bN0 NSCLC 14 days prior; persistent air leak via right apical chest tube (continuous bubbling at rest). CT chest fistulogram: air leak localized to right upper lobe bronchial stump region (B2 stump). Balloon occlusion test: occlusion of RUL bronchus (anterior and posterior segments) confirmed cessation of bubbling.

PROCEDURE: Flexible bronchoscope introduced orally. RUL bronchus assessed: stump intact at surgical anastomosis; B1 and B3 patent; B2 posterior orifice with small communication — air egress confirmed by saline-bubble test. Three Spiration IBV valves (6 mm, 7 mm, 5.5 mm) placed under fluoroscopic guidance into RUL B1 (6mm), RUL B3 (7mm), RUL B2 (5.5mm). Post-placement inspection: valves well-seated, Heimlich-flutter mechanism patent. Bubble test post-placement: cessation of air leak confirmed.

Note: patientpatient [sic] was repositioned during procedure — corrected documentation: patient repositioned to right lateral decubitus briefly for fluoroscopic valve placement in B3.

SPECIMENS: None.

COMPLICATIONS: None. EBL: None. Air leak cessation confirmed intraoperatively.

PLAN:
1. Chest tube to water seal; if output <200 mL/24h and no air leak, clamp trial and remove within 24h.
2. Repeat CXR in 12h.
3. If valve not tolerated (cough, infection): removal planned at 6 weeks if fistula sealed, or earlier if symptomatic.
4. Thoracic surgery follow-up.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

BRONCHOSCOPY PROCEDURE NOTE — FLEXIBLE DIAGNOSTIC
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 57M
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Suspected pulmonary tuberculosis; AFB smear positive x1 (A15.0 provisional)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; BAL and TBBX obtained for cultures and pathology

PROCEDURE: Flexible bronchoscopy, BAL (CPT 31624), transbronchial biopsy (CPT 31628)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation. Midazolam 1 mg + fentanyl 5 0 mcg [noisy unit — intended 50 mcg] IV. Topical lidocaine 4%, 6 mL total.

CLINICAL NOTE: Procedure performed in negative-pressure bronchoscopy suite. All staff in N95 respirators and full airborne precautions (TB protocol). [Nursing note cross-reference lists "positive pressure room" — confirmed via OR scheduler as negative pressure; documentation error in nursing note.]

AIRWAY FINDINGS:
Patient positioned supine. Bronchoscope introduced via nostril (nasal approach — patient preference). Vocal cords visualized; normal movement. Trachea: erythematous mucosa, mild, diffuse. Carina sharp. Right upper lobe: mucosal erythema and mild friability at RUL posterior segment (B2); no discrete mass; no caseous material visible. Left mainstem and lower lobe airways: mild diffuse mucosal erythema; no discrete lesion.

Concentrated sampling from RUL B2 (highest suspicion based on CT and sputum smear localization):
  BAL: 60 mL saline, 25 mL return — sent for AFB smear and culture (MGIT liquid culture), fungal culture, cytology, Legionella/pneumococcal PCR.
  TBBX: 4 transbronchial biopsies from RUL B2 under fluoroscopic guidance — formalin; AFB stain on tissue, PCR for MTB.

SPECIMENS:
  A: RUL B2 BAL — AFB (smear + MGIT), fungal, bacterial Cx, cytology, Legionella/pneumococcal PCR
  B: RUL B2 TBBX x4 — formalin; H&E, AFB stain, MTB PCR

COMPLICATIONS: Mild diffuse mucosal hemorrhage post-biopsy; controlled with cold saline lavage. EBL: ~3 mL.

PLAN:
1. Strict AFB isolation continued.
2. Initiate 4-drug RIPE therapy empirically pending culture results (ID co-management).
3. Public health notification completed per protocol.
4. Results in 48h (rapid PCR) and 6–8 weeks (culture).

NOTE TEXT:

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, copy_paste_artifacts

BRONCHOSCOPY / LINEAR EBUS NOTE
PERFORMING PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 55F
DATE: [REDACTED]

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Mediastinal lymphadenopathy (R59.9); endobronchial lesion right upper lobe bronchus; biopsy results pending.

PLAN (stated early per template):
- Await pathology and cytology results (5–7 business days)
- If adenocarcinoma confirmed: molecular reflex testing (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, KRAS G12C, MET, NTRK, PD-L1) already ordered as reflex on specimen
- Oncology referral placed
- Repeat CT chest in 2 months if non-diagnostic

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: New 3.2 cm RUL mass with mediastinal adenopathy; rule out malignancy

PROCEDURES: Flexible bronchoscopy with EBUS-TBNA; endobronchial biopsy

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation (midazolam 3 mg, fentanyl 100 mcg)

INSTRUMENTS: Olympus BF-UC180F EBUS scope; Olympus BF-H190 therapeutic scope (switched for endobronchial biopsy)

CLINICAL BACKGROUND: 55-year-old 55-year-old [sic] female, 35 pack-year smoking history, presenting with 3.2 cm spiculated RUL mass on CT, PET-avid (SUVmax 12.4); PET also demonstrated FDG uptake in stations 4R and 7 (SUVmax 3.1 and 2.8 respectively).

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
EBUS introduced transorally. Survey of mediastinum:
  Station 2R: Not clearly visualized.
  Station 4R: 1.6 cm node, heterogeneous, TBNA x3 passes. ROSE: malignant cells, consistent with adenocarcinoma.
  Station 7: 1.1 cm node, homogeneous; TBNA x2 passes. ROSE: atypical cells.
  Station 10R: 0.8 cm, TBNA x2 passes. ROSE: lymphocytes only.

Scope exchanged for therapeutic bronchoscope. RUL anterior segment (B3): polypoid 8 mm endobronchial lesion visualized; biopsy forceps x4 passes; moderate ooze, controlled with 1:10,000 epinephrine 2 mL topical instillation. Brushings x2.

SPECIMENS:
  A: Station 4R — 3 passes, cytology + cell block
  B: Station 7 — 2 passes, cytology
  C: Station 10R — 2 passes, cytology
  D: RUL B3 endobronchial biopsy x4 — formalin
  E: RUL B3 brushings x2 — ThinPrep

COMPLICATIONS: Minor hemorrhage at endobronchial biopsy site; controlled with topical epinephrine. EBL: ~3 mL.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: inconsistent_formatting

PROCEDURE NOTE
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   AGE/SEX: 61F   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD

DIAGNOSIS (pre): Mucus plugging with atelectasis, right lower lobe (J98.11); ventilator-dependent respiratory failure (J96.00)

DIAGNOSIS (post): Same; mucus clearance achieved, atelectasis improved

PROCEDURE: Flexible bronchoscopy, diagnostic and therapeutic (CPT 31622; 31623 — BAL/culture)

INDICATION: Mechanically ventilated patient, ICU day 7; right lower lobe opacification on portable CXR; increasing ventilator pressures (peak 38, plateau 29 cm H2O); suspected mucus plugging.

ANESTHESIA: No additional sedation beyond baseline ICU infusions (fentanyl + propofol). Paralytic held. PEEP adjusted to 8 cm H2O during procedure.

TECHNIQUE:
Elbow adapter placed on ETT (size 8.0). Flexible bronchoscope (Olympus BF-P290, 4.9 mm OD, 2.0 mm working channel) introduced via adapter. SpO2 maintained >88% (FiO2 1.0 during procedure). PEEP 8 cm H2O.

Findings:
    RIGHT — mucus plug visualized at RLL bronchus intermedius/RLL junction. Large tan-brown mucoid cast occupying 80% of lumen. Forceps used for piecemeal extraction of plug (3 passes). Saline lavage 20 mL x3 aliquots with suctioning. Post-clearance: lumen patent; B6, B7, B8 all patent.
    LEFT — patent throughout; no mucus plug.

BAL: 60 mL instilled into RLL, 25 mL return — sent for Gram stain, culture, sensitivity, fungal.
BAL cellular differential ordered.

Post-procedure: Ventilator peak pressure improved to 27 cm H2O. SpO2 94% on FiO2 0.6 (down from 1.0 pre-procedure, over 30 min).

SPECIMENS: RLL BAL — micro, cytology

COMPLICATIONS: Transient SpO2 drop to 84% during plug extraction (recovered immediately with FiO2 increase). No pneumothorax. EBL: None.

PLAN:
1. Increase mucolytic therapy (acetylcysteine via ETT q8h).
2. Elevate HOB 30–45 degrees.
3. Repeat CXR in 4 hours.
4. Return bronchoscopy if re-plugging suspected.

3. ENT notified of trach placement as requested by family.
4. Goals-of-care discussion to follow placement; palliative medicine consultation requested.

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; percutaneous trach placed.

PROCEDURE: PDT (CPT 31600; 31622)

MONTGOMERY T-TUBE INSERTION — OPERATIVE NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD / CO-SURGEON: [NAME:REDACTED], MD (ENT)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 52M

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Post-cricoid and upper tracheal stenosis, grade II (Cotton-Myer), idiopathic (J95.5)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; T-tube placed, airway patent

PROCEDURE: Rigid bronchoscopy + Nd:YAG laser + balloon dilation + Montgomery T-tube insertion

ANESTHESIA: GA, TIVA, spontaneous respiration maintained via rigid bronchoscope.

STENT SELECTION TABLE:

	Parameter	Measurement	T-tube Selection
	CT tracheal lumen at stenosis	7 mm	13 mm inner diameter
	Stenosis length	2.4 cm	70 mm tracheal limb
	Subglottis to stenosis	1.5 cm	Proximal limb: 20 mm (above cords)
	Planned stoma level	Ring 3–4	30 mm sidearm, right-angle exit

PROCEDURE SUMMARY:
Rigid bronchoscopy confirmed grade II circumferential upper tracheal stenosis. Nd:YAG laser web radial incisions (12/3/6/9 o'clock). Serial balloon dilation to 13 mm. Stoma created at ring 3–4. T-tube (13 × 70 × 30 mm) folded and introduced via rigid barrel; upper limb seated above cords under laryngoscopic guidance; sidearm exteriorized through stoma. Position confirmed on flexible bronchoscopy: lumen patent, carina 4 cm below distal end. Phonation test: intelligible voice with sidearm plugged.

COMPLICATIONS: Minor hemorrhage at stoma incision site; controlled with direct pressure. Grade I mucosal laceration (radial laser sites). EBL: 8 mL.

PLAN: T-tube care protocol; ENT/IP co-follow-up; SLP evaluation. Planned duration 12 months minimum.

NOTE: note_010

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: table_with_tabs, missing_section

OPERATIVE REPORT — AIRWAY STENT PLACEMENT
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 68M

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Malignant tracheal stenosis; non-small cell lung cancer, right lung (C34.10); extrinsic airway compression

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; Y-stent placed successfully, airway patency restored

PROCEDURES:
  31631 — Bronchoscopy, rigid, with stent placement
  CPT 31641 — Therapeutic bronchoscopy (APC, pre-stent debulking)

ANESTHESIA: General anesthesia; jet ventilation

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
68-year-old male with known NSCLC (previously treated with chemoradiation), now with progressive dyspnea and stridor. CT: distal tracheal narrowing 60%, carina involvement with bilateral mainstem compression. Discussed in multidisciplinary conference; palliation with Y-stent recommended.

Patient intubated; switched to jet ventilation via Karl Storz 12.5 mm rigid bronchoscope. Tracheal lumen at carina 40% of expected (critical stenosis). Tumor fronds at carina debrided with rigid forceps and APC (45 W). Mucosal hemostasis confirmed.

Dumon Y-stent sizing:
	Component	Inner Diameter	Length	Position
	Tracheal limb	18 mm	40 mm	Distal trachea
	Right bronchial limb	14 mm	25 mm	RMB
	Left bronchial limb	12 mm	20 mm	LMB

Stent deployed via stent pusher through rigid barrel; repositioned once for optimal carina coverage. Post-deployment flexible bronchoscopy through rigid barrel confirmed:
  - Tracheal limb: well-seated, mucosa visible proximal to stent, distal opening at carina
  - Right limb: seated in RMB, RUL orifice visible and patent
  - Left limb: seated in LMB, LUL and LLL orifices patent
  Immediate post-stent airway patency: >90%

COMPLICATIONS: None. EBL: <10 mL.

PLAN:
1. Humidified air/O2 to prevent mucus plugging; saline nebulization TID.
2. Stent surveillance bronchoscopy in 6 weeks.
3. Oncology follow-up for systemic therapy options.
4. Patient and family counseled re: palliative intent.

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: missing_section, unicode_checkboxes

SMALL-BORE CHEST TUBE / PLEURAL DRAINAGE NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE: 65M

INDICATION: Right-sided parapneumonic effusion, complicated (pH 7.18, LDH 2,400, glucose 42); empyema suspected (J86.9)

PRE-PROCEDURE CHECKLIST:
  ☑ Consent obtained
  ☑ Coags reviewed: INR 1.2, Plt 210k
  ☑ Ultrasound: moderate loculated R effusion confirmed 0‌ posterior location 1‌
  ☐ CT chest: available but not repeated (reviewed previous from 2 days prior)
  ☑ Time-out completed

PROCEDURE:
Patient positioned right side up, left lateral decubitus. US survey: loculated right effusion, septated, estimated 400 mL; best window 7th ICS posterior axillary line. Site marked, prepped, draped. Local anesthetic: lidocaine 1% 10 mL. Seldinger technique used; 0.035-inch guidewire placed into pleural space under real-time US guidance; serial dilation; 14 Fr Seldinger-type chest tube (Rocket Medical) placed and secured with 2-0 nylon suture + adhesive dressing. Connected to Pleur-evac water-seal drainage at -20 cm H2O.

Initial output: 80 mL thick turbid yellow-green fluid (consistent with empyema). Tube flushed with 20 mL NS. Position confirmed on portable CXR: tube coursing posterolaterally, tip at right apex.

INTRAPLEURAL FIBRINOLYTIC THERAPY PLAN (to begin tomorrow morning):
  t-PA 10 mg in 30 mL NS + DNase 5 mg in 30 mL NS, instilled sequentially, dwell time 1 hour, BID x3 days per MIST2-based protocol.

SPECIMENS: Initial pleural aspirate 80 mL — Gram stain, culture, AFB, pH 7.18 (confirmed), LDH, protein, cytology

COMPLICATIONS: None. Mild pleuritic pain during insertion (expected); analgesic (ketorolac 15 mg IV) administered.

PLAN:
1. Monitor tube output; goal drainage >250 mL/day.
2. Begin fibrinolytic protocol AM.
3. CT chest in 48–72 h to assess loculation resolution.
4. ID consult for antibiotic guidance.
5. Surgery consult if fails to drain adequately at 72h.

================================================================================
NOTE: note_035
================================================================================

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, extreme_length

OPERATIVE REPORT — MONTGOMERY T-TUBE INSERTION
INSTITUTION: [NAME:REDACTED] Medical Center
DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP, DAABIP
CO-ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD (ENT/Head & Neck Surgery)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 44F

PLAN AND CLINICAL DECISION SUMMARY (predocumented per case conference note):
Multidisciplinary airway conference (IP + ENT + anesthesia) concluded: patient is not a surgical candidate for tracheal resection-reconstruction at this time (BMI 47, FEV1 38% predicted, significant medical comorbidities). Montgomery T-tube insertion selected as definitive long-term airway stent for post-intubation complex subglottic-tracheal stenosis. Patient counseled extensively. Consent obtained. Plan: T-tube insertion via rigid bronchoscopy + direct laryngoscopy under GA. Long-term management with IP and ENT co-follow-up. Decannulation goal deferred until weight loss and lung function optimized.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS:
  1. Post-intubation subglottic tracheal stenosis, complex, grade III (Cotton-Myer) (J95.5)
  2. Tracheomalacia, coexisting, distal to stenotic segment
  3. Morbid obesity (BMI 47) with obesity hypoventilation syndrome

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; Montgomery T-tube (13 mm × 70 mm × 30 mm sidearm) in satisfactory position; airway patent.

PROCEDURES:
  31631     — Rigid bronchoscopy
  31630     — Balloon dilation (pre-T-tube)
  31641     — Therapeutic bronchoscopy (Nd:YAG laser, web incision)
  [T-tube insertion — unlisted airway procedure with CPT to be confirmed with coding team; T-tube-specific stenting CPT per coder recommendation]

ANESTHESIA: GA; TIVA (propofol/remifentanil infusion). Spontaneous ventilation maintained via rigid bronchoscope — no paralytic (required for voice assessment post-procedure). Anesthesiologist: [NAME:REDACTED], MD.

CLINICAL BACKGROUND:
44-year-old female intubated for 22 days (4 months prior) for ARDS complicating influenza pneumonia. Extubated; noted dyspnea and biphasic stridor 6 weeks post-extubation. CT trachea: circumferential subglottic stenosis beginning 1.5 cm below cords; length 3.2 cm; estimated lumen 4–5 mm. Cotton-Myer grade III (>70% narrowing). Flexible bronchoscopy confirmed mature fibrous web with distal tracheomalacia segment (3 cm, >50% dynamic expiratory collapse at RMB takeoff). Pulmonary function: severe mixed obstructive/restrictive pattern; FEV1 38%. Multiple balloon dilations in the past 3 months (x3 sessions); restenosis each time within 4–6 weeks. Decision for T-tube as bridge.

INSTRUMENTS:
  - Karl Storz rigid bronchoscopes (8.5, 10.5, 12.5 mm, sequential)
  - Olympus BF-P180 flexible bronchoscope (for distal survey and post-placement check)
  - Montgomery T-tube (Boston Medical Products): selected size 13 mm inner diameter (tracheal limb), 70 mm tracheal limb length, 30 mm sidearm — sizing based on pre-procedure CT tracheal measurements
  - Nd:YAG laser (1064 nm)
  - CRE balloon (10–12–14 mm)
  - T-tube introducer (dedicated Montgomery T-tube forceps)

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:

PHASE 1 — RIGID BRONCHOSCOPY, ASSESSMENT, AND DILATION:
Rigid bronchoscope (8.5 mm) introduced transorally under direct laryngoscopy. Stenotic segment engaged at 1.5 cm below cords; lumen 4–5 mm (just accommodated 8.5 mm scope passage with tactile resistance). Web appeared mature, fibrous, white. Distal trachea post-stenosis: mild tracheomalacia confirmed. Carina and bilateral mainstem bronchi patent; no distal disease.

Nd:YAG laser (20 W, 0.3 sec pulses) applied at 12, 3, 6, and 9 o'clock radially within the stenotic web (total ~50 pulses). Radial incisions made through fibrous tissue; lumen progressively opened. Scope exchanged to 10.5 mm; passed through dilated lumen. Balloon (10 mm, then 12 mm, then 14 mm) serially inflated: each inflation 45–60 seconds at 6–8 atm. Grade I mucosal lacerations at all four radial incision sites (expected). Post-dilation lumen estimated 13 mm — adequate for T-tube placement.

PHASE 2 — T-TUBE SIZING AND SELECTION:
T-tube selected (13 mm × 70 mm × 30 mm sidearm, per pre-procedure planning). Upper limb (proximal) length confirmed to extend to level of vocal cords without contact. Sidearm length: accommodates anterior tracheal stoma (marked at ring 3–4 interspace for sidearm exit). Stoma creation: small stab incision (1.5 cm) at ring 3–4 interspace made with 15-blade, guided by rigid bronchoscope transillumination.

PHASE 3 — T-TUBE INSERTION:
T-tube folded using dedicated forceps (Montgomery T-tube insertion forceps). Tube folded in a U-configuration; loaded into barrel of 12.5 mm rigid scope. T-tube advanced distally through rigid barrel into trachea; positioned below stenotic segment. Upper limb unfolded and maneuvered proximally past the vocal cord level using grasping forceps under direct laryngoscopic visualization. Sidearm delivered through anterior stoma incision under bronchoscopic transillumination guidance. Tube alignment confirmed:
  - Upper limb (proximal): extends 1.0 cm above cords, no contact with arytenoids
  - Tracheal limb (distal): 3.5 cm above carina; spans entire stenotic and malacic segment
  - Sidearm: exits anteriorly at ring 3–4; plugged with provided stopper

Flexible bronchoscopy through T-tube lumen (sidearm plugged): lumen patent throughout; no kinking; posterior wall intact; bilateral mainstem bronchi confirmed patent distally.

Phonation test: sidearm uncapped; patient asked to vocalize — intelligible phonation confirmed (patient conscious, GA lightened briefly for vocal test; anesthesiologist confirmed adequate depth otherwise). Sidearm re-plugged.

T-tube secured to skin: 2-0 silk suture around sidearm and sutured to anterior skin margin x2; adhesive dressing.

SPECIMENS: None.

COMPLICATIONS: Grade I mucosal lacerations at laser/dilation sites (expected); hemostasis achieved. Transient SpO2 83% during forceps maneuver of proximal limb past cords — FiO2 increased; recovered to 96% within 90 seconds. EBL: ~12 mL. No arrhythmia. No airway fire.

POST-PROCEDURE PLAN:
1. T-tube care: sidearm cleaning BID; saline nebulization QID.
2. ENT/IP co-follow-up in 2 weeks; flexible bronchoscopy through sidearm to confirm position.
3. Speech therapy and swallowing assessment — T-tube allows voicing when sidearm plugged.
4. Sidearm left plugged unless acute respiratory distress; emergency protocol reviewed with patient and family.
5. Target duration: 12–18 months (or until surgical candidacy improves).
6. Weight loss program referral; bariatric surgery consultation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: copy_paste_artifacts, inconsistent_formatting, malformed_codes

PROCEDURE NOTE — THORACENTESIS WITH COMPLICATION
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 69F

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Left-sided pleural effusion, malignant, recurrent (J91.0); breast cancer with pleural mets (C79.2)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; thoracentesis completed; re-expansion pulmonary edema (complication)

PROCEDURE CPT: 32555-71 [modifier 71: bilateral procedure, unilateral performed — note: modifier application under review by billing; format 32555:71 confirmed incorrect for this case per coder; pending correction]

ANESTHESIA: Local only; 1% lidocaine 8 mL.

PROCEDURE: US-guided left thoracentesis; 17-gauge Turkel kit; large anechoic effusion confirmed on US (depth 6.1 cm). Drainage initiated; 1,650 mL straw-colored fluid drained. Patient complained of progressive chest tightness beginning at 1,200 mL — drainage continued per patient request ("I want it all out"). At 1,650 mL, patient developed acute respiratory distress: SpO2 dropped from 97% to 84%; new right-sided rales on auscultation; patient coughing vigorously.

COMPLICATION — RE-EXPANSION PULMONARY EDEMA:
Drainage stopped immediately at 1,650 mL. Patient placed in sitting position; supplemental O2 increased from 2 L NC to 15 L NRB. CXR portable: pulmonary edema pattern in left (re-expanded) lung consistent with re-expansion pulmonary edema (REPE). patientpatient [sic] — patient monitored closely; SpO2 improved to 94% over 20 minutes on NRB. Diuresis: furosemide 40 mg IV per primary team.

Admission for monitoring; telemetry. SpO2 97% on 2 L NC by hour 4.

SPECIMENS: Pleural fluid 1,650 mL — cytology, LDH, protein, pH, CEA.

COMPLICATIONS: Re-expansion pulmonary edema; O2-responsive; no intubation required. Adverse event documented. EBL: None.

PLAN:
1. Future thoracenteses capped at 1,000–1,200 mL per session.
2. Oncology re: systemic therapy optimization to reduce effusion rate.
3. Indwelling pleural catheter (IPC) discussion for symptom palliation — appointment scheduled.

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, inconsistent_formatting

PROCEDURE NOTE — NAVIGATIONAL BRONCHOSCOPY WITH COMPLICATION
TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX POST-CRYOBIOPSY — EMERGENT MANAGEMENT
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 54F

FINAL DISPOSITION: Patient recovered, chest tube placed, pneumothorax resolved, admitted overnight for observation. Biopsy results pending.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Right lower lobe 2.1 cm solid nodule, PET-positive (SUVmax 6.1), newly detected (R91.1)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; post-procedural tension pneumothorax (complication of cryobiopsy) managed; 14 Fr chest tube placed

PROCEDURES:
  31627 — Navigational bronchoscopy
  31628 — Transbronchial biopsy (cryoprobe)
  [Needle aspiration/chest tube: 32551 — tube thoracostomy, emergent]

ANESTHESIA: GA; ETT; TIVA.

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
Robotic navigational bronchoscopy to RLL posterior basal segment (B10); shape-sensing confirmed; rEBUS: eccentric ring (adjacent to lesion). Cryoprobe deployed: 3 freeze-thaw cycles, 2 seconds each. Tissue cores adequate x3. Brushings x2. BAL 50 mL.

COMPLICATION — TENSION PNEUMOTHORAX:
During scope withdrawal, SpO2 acutely declined from 99% to 74% with rising peak airway pressures (peak 52 cm H2O, plateau 47 cm H2O). Tracheal deviation noted on clinical inspection (toward left). Breath sounds absent on right. Diagnosis: right tension pneumothorax.

EMERGENT DECOMPRESSION:
  T+0 — Tension pneumothorax recognized.
  T+1:00 — 14-gauge angiocath inserted at right 2nd ICS, MCL (needle decompression); rush of air confirmed; SpO2 improved to 88%; airway pressures fell to peak 36.
  T+2:00 — 14 Fr small-bore chest tube placed (Seldinger) at right 5th ICS, anterior axillary line; connected to water-seal drainage.
  T+3:00 — SpO2 99%; airway pressures normalized (peak 26, plateau 20).
  CXR: right lung re-expanded; chest tube in satisfactory position.

Patient extubated uneventfully 45 minutes post-chest tube placement. Admitted to step-down unit.

SPECIMENS: RLL cryobiopsies x3 — formalin. Brushings — ThinPrep. BAL — cytology, culture.

COMPLICATIONS: Right tension pneumothorax — managed emergently as above. EBL: <5 mL (separate from pneumothorax event). Adverse event report filed.

PLAN:
1. Chest tube to water seal; remove when output <100 mL/24h and no air leak x24h.
2. Repeat CXR q12h.
3. Pathology results in 5–7 days.
4. Oncology referral if malignant.
5. Case to be presented at IP quality review.

================================================================================
NOTE: note_041
================================================================================

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: extreme_length, inconsistent_formatting, conflicting_details

OPERATIVE REPORT — PROCEDURE WITH SIGNIFICANT COMPLICATION
EBUS-TBNA COMPLICATED BY MASSIVE HEMOPTYSIS AND EMERGENT AIRWAY INTERVENTION
DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP, DAABIP
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 67M

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Right hilar mass (3.8 cm), highly PET-avid; mediastinal adenopathy; suspected NSCLC (R91.1; R59.9)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: EBUS-TBNA performed; massive hemoptysis post-biopsy requiring emergent rigid bronchoscopy and airway tamponade; NSCLC suspected (cytology pending)

PROCEDURES:
  31652 — EBUS-TBNA, 2 stations
  31641 — Emergent rigid bronchoscopy, therapeutic (hemorrhage control)
  31620 — Endobronchial ultrasound (guidance)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation → converted to GA (emergent). Moderate sedation initiated: midazolam 4 mg + fentanyl 125 mcg IV. Emergent GA: propofol 150 mg IV bolus + succinylcholine 120 mg IV for intubation.

CLINICAL BACKGROUND:
67-year-old male, anticoagulated on apixaban (held 48h pre-procedure — patient self-reported; pharmacy verification: last dose confirmed 36 hours prior, not 48h [CONFLICT: intake form documents "48h hold" confirmed; medication reconciliation shows 36h — subtherapeutic wash-out; noted retrospectively]). Known NSCLC suspicion. INR 1.0, platelets 198k (labs drawn morning of procedure).

PROCEDURE (Antecedent to Complication):
EBUS bronchoscope introduced. Station 4R: 1.9 cm node; 3 passes; ROSE: malignant (adenocarcinoma). Station 10R: 2.2 cm hilar node; 4 passes (needle advanced into friable vascular-appearing node). On fourth pass at station 10R, brisk red blood appeared in suction tubing followed by patient cough spike. Assessment: vigorous suction — blood visible pooling in right mainstem bronchus via EBUS scope.

COMPLICATION — MASSIVE HEMOPTYSIS:
Volume assessment: approximately 150–200 mL bright red blood suctioned within 3 minutes; continued active hemorrhage from RMB. SpO2 fell from 97% to 78% over 90 seconds. Decision: emergent conversion to general anesthesia.

EMERGENT MANAGEMENT SEQUENCE:
  T+0:00 — Hemorrhage recognized; suction maximized; patient repositioned right lateral decubitus (bleeding side down) to protect left lung
  T+0:45 — Code airway called; anesthesia to bedside
  T+1:30 — Propofol 150 mg IV + succinylcholine 120 mg IV; direct laryngoscopy; 8.0 ETT placed; intubated. SpO2 82%.
  T+2:00 — Rigid bronchoscope (Karl Storz 12.5 mm) brought to room emergently. ETT removed; rigid scope introduced.
  T+3:00 — Rigid scope in RMB: blood clot and active hemorrhage from RMB/station 10R region visualized. Clot partially extracted with suction and rigid forceps. Active vessel not visualized (submucosal).
  T+4:00 — Fogarty balloon catheter (14 Fr) introduced through rigid scope into RMB; balloon inflated 3 mL to tamponade RMB. Contralateral (left) lung ventilated via rigid barrel/jet ventilation. SpO2 improved to 89%.
  T+6:00 — Epinephrine 1:10,000 solution 4 mL instilled through working channel alongside Fogarty.
  T+8:00 — Fogarty balloon deflated under direct vision: active hemorrhage diminished to slow ooze. Cold saline irrigation 20 mL x3. APC applied (2 L/min flow, 35 W) to hemorrhagic submucosal area at RMB medial wall (site of station 10R TBNA).
  T+12:00 — Hemostasis achieved; minimal ooze only. Fogarty removed. Flexible bronchoscope through rigid barrel: bilateral airways inspected; left lung clear; RML, RLL bronchi patent; residual blood-tinged secretions only.
  T+15:00 — 8.0 ETT placed through rigid barrel under bronchoscopic guidance; rigid scope removed; patient ventilated via ETT. Transferred to ICU intubated.

TOTAL BLOOD LOSS: Estimated 250–300 mL (suction collection measured 270 mL).

POST-PROCEDURE COURSE (documented in this note for continuity):
Patient transferred to ICU intubated. CT angiography chest performed post-stabilization: no active contrast extravasation; no pseudoaneurysm; RMB mucosal irregularity consistent with biopsy site. Interventional radiology placed on standby (not required). Apixaban not restarted. Patient extubated at ICU hour 14 after stabilization. CXR: mild RML/RLL haziness (aspiration of blood); improved at 24h.

FORMAL COMPLICATION CLASSIFICATION: Severe procedural complication (massive hemoptysis requiring emergent airway intervention and ICU admission). Adverse event report filed.

SPECIMENS: Station 4R (3 passes) — cytology + cell block. Station 10R specimens contaminated with blood — cytospin sent; pathology notified of hemorrhagic specimen.

PLAN:
1. Repeat EBUS-TBNA deferred minimum 4 weeks; if station 4R ROSE is diagnostic (adenocarcinoma), may not be needed.
2. Apixaban discussion with cardiology re: restart timing.
3. CT-guided biopsy of station 10R hilar node as alternative if tissue required.
4. Oncology referral with available cytology results.
5. Formal M&M case review scheduled.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: table_with_tabs, ambiguous_laterality

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY PROCEDURE NOTE
INSTITUTION: [NAME:REDACTED] Medical Center
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [REDACTED]
PERFORMING PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
FELLOW PRESENT: [NAME:REDACTED], MD (2nd year PCCM)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]     MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]
AGE/SEX: 71-year-old male

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Left upper lobe pulmonary nodule, 2.8 cm (R91.1); mediastinal staging required

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Pending pathology and cytology results

PROCEDURE(S):
  31627  — Navigational bronchoscopy (electromagnetic/robotic guidance)
  31623  — Brushings
  31624  — BAL
  36000  — IV access (performed separately)

ANESTHESIA: General anesthesia; endotracheal intubation by anesthesia team. TIVA protocol.

INSTRUMENTS: Ion Robotic Bronchoscopy System (Intuitive Surgical); radial EBUS probe (1.4 mm, 20 MHz) via working channel; fluoroscopy C-arm available.

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
After induction of general anesthesia and placement of 8.5 Fr ETT, the Ion robotic bronchoscope was introduced transorally. White-light inspection confirmed normal bilateral vocal cord motion [note: left cord initially reported as reduced motion — confirmed normal on repeat pass; documentation corrected]. Trachea midline; carina sharp.

Robotic navigation plan loaded from preoperative CT chest (performed [REDACTED], nodule coordinates registered). Catheter navigated to right [sic] upper lobe target — operator note: target confirmed LEFT upper lobe, apical-posterior segment (LB1+2). Shape-sensing confirmation of catheter position achieved. Radial EBUS probe deployed through extended working channel (EWC): concentric ring pattern identified around target lesion, confirming probe within/adjacent to nodule. Fluoroscopic spot image confirmed position.

Samples obtained:
  Four transbronchial biopsies (cryoprobe, 1.1 mm, 3-second freeze cycles x4)
  Bronchial brushings x2 passes
  BAL: 60 mL instilled, 38 mL returned, from LUL (B1+2)

CT-on-rails not available at this facility; C-arm fluoroscopy used for orthogonal confirmation.

Nodule characteristics on radial EBUS:

	Target	Size (CT)	rEBUS signal	Passes
	LUL apical-posterior	2.8 cm	Concentric	4 cryobx + 2 brush
	LUL anterior (incidental small GGO, 6mm)	6 mm	Not targeted	—

Post-procedure chest X-ray ordered (portable, post-anesthesia recovery).

SPECIMENS:
  A1 — LUL cryobiopsies x4: formalin-fixed, routine H&E + IHC panel per pathology protocol
  A2 — LUL brushings: ThinPrep cytology
  A3 — LUL BAL: cell differential, AFB/fungal cultures, cytology

COMPLICATIONS: None observed intraoperatively. Monitoring for pneumothorax post-procedure.

IMPRESSION/PLAN:
1. Technically successful robotic navigational bronchoscopy with rEBUS confirmation of LUL 2.8 cm target.
2. Cryobiopsy x4 obtained; diagnostic yield anticipated high given concentric rEBUS sign.
3. Post-procedure CXR in recovery; if pneumothorax excluded, discharge per protocol.
4. Results to multidisciplinary lung nodule conference.

NOTE: note_008

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: malformed_codes, inconsistent_formatting

PROCEDURE NOTE
PATIENT: [PATIENT]  MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]  AGE/SEX: 59 year old male
DATE: [REDACTED]  ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED]

PRE-OP DIAGNOSIS: Post-intubation tracheal stenosis, subglottic, moderate-severe (J95.5)
POST-OP DIAGNOSIS: Same; dilation performed, improved lumen

PROCEDURES / CPT:
31630-22   Balloon bronchoplasty, trachea (increased complexity modifier — concurrent laser)
31641      Bronchoscopy w/ therapeutic intervention (laser)
  [Note: code formatting dispute — billing queries 31630:22 vs 31630-22; see modifier addendum]

ANESTHESIA: General anesthesia, TIVA, LMA #4

INSTRUMENTS:
- Olympus BF-P180 thin bronchoscope (4.9 mm OD) for initial assessment through LMA
- Nd:YAG laser fiber, 1064 nm, 20 W, 0.5 sec pulses
- CRE balloon, 12–14–16 mm progressive inflation
- Karl Storz rigid bronchoscope 10 mm for final dilation (passed after LMA removal)

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
59-year-old male with history of prolonged ICU intubation (21 days) for ARDS 8 months prior; progressive exertional dyspnea and stridor. CT trachea: circumferential subglottic stenosis 1.5 cm length, estimated 35–40% luminal patency. Pulmonary function: flow-volume loop with flattened inspiratory and expiratory limbs.

PROCEDURE:
LMA placed; flexible bronchoscope introduced. Subglottic web-like circumferential stenosis visualized 2 cm below cords; lumen estimated 4–5 mm diameter. No dynamic collapse. Nd:YAG laser applied radially at 12, 3, 6, and 9 o'clock positions along the stenotic segment (20 W, contact mode, ~40 pulses total); tissue ablation and incision of fibrous web achieved. Balloon (12 mm, then 14 mm) serially inflated to 6 atm, held 60 seconds each. LMA removed; rigid bronchoscope 10 mm inserted transorally — used as final dilator, pushed through stenotic segment under direct vision. Post-dilation lumen estimated 10–11 mm. Mucosal bleeding controlled with cold saline irrigation.

FINDINGS: Post-procedure airway patent, 10–11 mm estimated diameter. Grade I mucosal laceration at 6 o'clock — expected, acceptable. No cartilaginous injury. Distal airways unremarkable.

PLAN:
1. Repeat bronchoscopy 4–6 weeks; consider intralesional steroid injection at next session.
2. Reflux precautions (PPI). Voice therapy referral.
3. Pulmonary function testing 6 weeks post-procedure.
4. Long-term surveillance — risk of restenosis high with this subtype.

COMPLICATIONS: Grade I mucosal laceration (expected, hemostasis achieved). EBL: <5 mL.

NOTE: note_021

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: table_with_tabs, missing_section

PROCEDURE NOTE: EBUS-TBNA — SARCOIDOSIS STAGING
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 38F

INDICATION: Bilateral hilar and mediastinal adenopathy on CT, stage uncertain; ACE elevated; non-caseating granulomas suspected (R59.9; D86.0 provisional)

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Bilateral hilar and mediastinal adenopathy, etiology indeterminate
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Pending (granulomatous disease vs lymphoma vs malignancy)

PROCEDURE: Linear EBUS-TBNA (CPT 31652); BAL (CPT 31624)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation per IP protocol.

EBUS NODAL MAP:

	Station	Short axis	Echogenicity	Internal echo	Passes	ROSE result
	4R	2.0 cm	Hypoechoic	Heterogeneous	3	Granulomatous inflammation (non-necrotizing)
	7	2.6 cm	Hypoechoic	Homogeneous	4	Granulomatous inflammation
	10R	1.4 cm	Hypoechoic	—	3	Granulomatous inflammation
	10L	1.1 cm	Hypoechoic	—	2	Lymphocytes + granuloma fragments
	4L	1.0 cm	Hypoechoic	—	2	Insufficient on ROSE

Additional passes station 4L: 2 more passes after initial insufficiency → total 4 passes; repeat ROSE: granulomatous tissue.

BAL performed from right middle lobe (B4): 100 mL instilled, 62 mL return. Sent for CD4:CD8 ratio, AFB culture, fungal culture, cytology.

SPECIMENS:
  A — Station 4R x3 passes: cytology + cell block + AFB/fungal stains + flow cytometry hold
  B — Station 7 x4 passes: cytology + cell block
  C — Station 10R x3 passes: cytology
  D — Station 10L x2+2 passes: cytology
  E — Station 4L x4 passes: cytology + cell block
  F — RML BAL: CD4:CD8 ratio, AFB, fungal Cx, cytology

COMPLICATIONS: None. EBL: <1 mL.

PLAN:
1. ROSE consistent with non-caseating granulomatous inflammation at 4 of 5 sampled stations — highly suggestive of sarcoidosis.
2. Formal pathology, AFB cultures, and BAL CD4:CD8 ratio to follow.
3. Rheumatology and pulmonology co-management; ophthalmology evaluation for uveitis.
4. 24h urine calcium; serum calcium and ACE if not recently obtained.
5. Await tissue confirmation before initiating systemic corticosteroid therapy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: extreme_length, table_with_tabs, inconsistent_formatting

COMPLEX COMBINED PROCEDURE OPERATIVE REPORT
ENDOBRONCHIAL ULTRASOUND / EUS-B / NAVIGATIONAL BRONCHOSCOPY — TRIPLE MODALITY STAGING AND BIOPSY
PROCEDURE DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING SURGEON/BRONCHOSCOPIST: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP, DAABIP
FELLOW: [NAME:REDACTED], MD (PCCM fellow, Year 2, direct supervision)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]    MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]    DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]    AGE/SEX: 58-year-old male
INSTITUTION: [NAME:REDACTED] Medical Center

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSES:
  1. Right upper lobe 3.8 cm mass, highly PET-avid (SUVmax 16.3) (R91.1)
  2. Mediastinal adenopathy — stations 2R, 4R, 4L, 7 enlarged on CT/PET
  3. Left adrenal incidentaloma, PET-avid (SUVmax 4.2) (E27.40)
  4. Suspected stage IIIB NSCLC vs stage IV disease, restaging required

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSES:
  1–3: Same; tissue obtained from all targets; pending pathology
  4. If adrenal confirmed met: cM1b (stage IV); if N3 only: cIIIB

CPT CODES:
  31652 — EBUS-TBNA, one or two mediastinal and/or hilar lymph node stations
  31653 — EBUS-TBNA, three or more stations
  31627 — Navigational bronchoscopy
  31628 — TBBX, each lobe (x1)
  43232 — EUS-B with FNA (transesophageal, EBUS scope — biopsy of adrenal)

ANESTHESIA: General anesthesia; LMA #5 for EBUS/ENB phases; TIVA (propofol 120 mcg/kg/min, remifentanil 0.1 mcg/kg/min). Anesthesiologist: [NAME:REDACTED], MD. Airway exchanged to 8.0 ETT for EUS-B transesophageal phase.

MONITORING: Arterial line (radial, left). Standard ASA monitors. Total procedure time: 2 hours 14 minutes.

INSTRUMENTS:
  - Olympus BF-UC180F linear EBUS (for endobronchial stations + transesophageal)
  - Medtronic SuperDimension ENB v7 for peripheral navigation
  - Olympus BF-P290 thin bronchoscope (4.9 mm) for peripheral phase
  - Radial EBUS probe 20 MHz, 2.0 mm
  - Vizishot 2, 21-gauge TBNA needle
  - 19-gauge EUS-FNA needle (transesophageal adrenal access)

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
58-year-old male, 40 pack-year smoking history, presenting with 3-month history of progressive cough, 15-pound weight loss, and hemoptysis. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: 3.8 cm RUL mass with spiculated margins; enlarged mediastinal nodes (2R 1.8 cm, 4R 2.2 cm, 4L 1.1 cm, station 7 2.4 cm); 2.1 cm left adrenal mass (PET SUVmax 4.2). PET-CT: hypermetabolic primary RUL mass; mediastinal and left adrenal involvement. MRI brain negative for metastases. Case presented at multidisciplinary lung cancer conference; plan for triple-modality staging bronchoscopy.

PROCEDURE NARRATIVE:

PHASE 1 — ENDOBRONCHIAL ULTRASOUND (EBUS-TBNA):
EBUS scope introduced via LMA. White-light survey: trachea patent, carina mildly widened (consistent with subcarinal adenopathy). Right and left mainstem bronchi patent; no endobronchial lesion identified at this phase.

Systematic EBUS-TBNA sampling (see table below):

	Station	Short-axis (cm)	Echogenicity	Borders	Passes	ROSE
	2R	1.8	Hypoechoic	Regular	3	Atypical/malignant
	4R	2.2	Hypoechoic, hetero	Irregular	4	Malignant (adenoca morphology)
	4L	1.1	Hypoechoic	Regular	3	Lymphocytes, atypical
	7	2.4	Hypo, central necrosis	Irregular	4	Malignant
	10R	0.7	Not sampled	—	0	—
	11L	Not visualized	—	—	0	—

Total TBNA passes: 14 passes across 4 stations.

Doppler confirmed: no intervening vessels at any sampled station.

PHASE 2 — EUS-B (TRANSESOPHAGEAL — LEFT ADRENAL):
LMA removed; 8.0 ETT placed by anesthesia. EBUS scope introduced transorally into esophagus (EUS-B technique). Left adrenal gland identified in transesophageal window posterior to left lobe of liver/gastroesophageal junction region. Adrenal gland: 2.1 cm heterogeneous mass with preserved adrenal architecture on periphery. 19-gauge FNA needle introduced through working channel; adrenal mass accessed under real-time EUS guidance x3 passes. ROSE: malignant cells, morphology consistent with adenocarcinoma metastasis.

PHASE 3 — NAVIGATIONAL BRONCHOSCOPY (PERIPHERAL LUNG MASS):
ETT retained. Scope exchanged to thin bronchoscope + ENB catheter. RUL posterior segment (B2) target registered from pre-operative CT (3.8 cm mass). Navigational guidance confirmed catheter position at target. Radial EBUS deployed: concentric ring, within lesion confirmed. CXR-equivalent fluoroscopy spot obtained.

Samples: 4 transbronchial biopsies (1.8 mm cup forceps); brushings x2; BAL 50 mL (30 mL return).

Post-procedure flexible bronchoscopy: RUL B2 minimal ooze, self-limited. No endobronchial lesion visible. Distal airways clear.

SPECIMENS:
	Label	Station/Site	Technique	Container/Destination
	A1	Station 2R	EBUS-TBNA x3	Cytology + cell block
	A2	Station 4R	EBUS-TBNA x4	Cytology + cell block + mol hold
	A3	Station 4L	EBUS-TBNA x3	Cytology + cell block
	A4	Station 7	EBUS-TBNA x4	Cytology + cell block + mol hold
	B1	Left adrenal	EUS-B FNA x3	Cytology + IHC (TTF-1, NapsinA, CK7, CK20)
	C1	RUL B2 mass	TBBX x4	Formalin, H&E + IHC
	C2	RUL B2 brushings	2 passes	ThinPrep cytology
	C3	RUL B2 BAL	50 mL	Cytology + culture

COMPLICATIONS: None significant. Estimated blood loss: <5 mL total. Post-procedure SpO2 97% on 2 L NC.

POST-PROCEDURE PLAN:
1. ROSE indicates malignancy at 4R, 7, and adrenal — highly suggestive of stage IV NSCLC with adrenal metastasis.
2. Await formal pathology and molecular profiling (NGS — EGFR/ALK/ROS1/KRAS/MET/NTRK/BRAF; PD-L1 22C3).
3. Present at next multidisciplinary thoracic oncology conference.
4. If adenocarcinoma confirmed: initiate work-up for targeted therapy vs immunotherapy based on molecular results.
5. Bone scan if PD-L1 high or if bone pain develops.
6. Palliative care consultation given stage IV provisional diagnosis.
7. Patient and family counseling regarding preliminary findings performed pre-discharge.

NOTE: note_004

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: conflicting_details, noisy_units

OPERATIVE REPORT
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]
DATE: [REDACTED]
SURGEON/BRONCHOSCOPIST: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Malignant central airway obstruction, right mainstem bronchus; squamous cell carcinoma (C34.11)
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same; post-debulking airway patency restored

PROCEDURES:
1. Rigid bronchoscopy
2. Mechanical debulking of endobronchial tumor
3. Electrocautery/snare tumor ablation
4. Argon plasma coagulation (APC)
5. Balloon bronchoplasty, 14 mm
CPT: 31641 (therapeutic bronchoscopy, tumor)

ANESTHESIA: General anesthesia [note: moderate sedation listed on nursing intake form — confirmed GA by anesthesia record; intake form error]. Jet ventilation via rigid bronchoscope. Anesthesiologist: [NAME:REDACTED], MD.

PROCEDURE NOTE:
Patient brought to the bronchoscopy suite in supine position. Induction of GA and muscle relaxation achieved. Rigid bronchoscope (Karl Storz 12.5 mm) introduced transorally; ETT not placed (jet ventilation used). Supraglottic structures normal. Tracheal lumen patent, approximately 85% of predicted cross-section. At the level of the right mainstem bronchus (RMB), a large polypoid friable tumor was identified occupying approximately 70% of the lumen, extending to the right upper lobe (RUL) bronchus takeoff. Left mainstem and bilateral upper and lower lobe bronchi patent without lesion.

Debulking initiated using rigid bronchoscope tip (core-out technique). Large tumor fragments extracted using optical forceps. Electrocautery snare applied to tumor stalk; power setting 40 W cut/coag. APC applied to bleeding mucosa at 2 L/min flow, 45 W; hemostasis achieved. Post-debulking, 14 mm CRE balloon inflated to 4 atm for 60 seconds at RMB; lumen re-expanded to approximately 80% predicted cross-section. Residual distal disease noted at RUL orifice; partial debulking only — discussed with patient/family pre-procedure.

Flexible bronchoscope (Olympus BF-H190) passed through rigid barrel for distal inspection: RUL superior segment patent, residual tumor fronds at orifice; RML and RLL patent. Secretions cleared with suction.

Estimated blood loss: 25–30 mL (suctioned and irrigated specimen). Epinephrine 1:20,000 solution 4m mg [sic] topically applied to bleeding sites — intended 4 mL of 1:20,000 solution; documented as administered by nursing.

SPECIMENS: Tumor fragments (multiple) from RMB debulking — permanent pathology for margins and molecular/IHC. Previously known SCC by prior biopsy.

COMPLICATIONS: Moderate hemorrhage, controlled intraoperatively with epinephrine and APC. No pneumothorax. No airway fire.

IMPRESSION/PLAN:
1. Successful debulking of RMB obstructing SCC; lumen restored to ~80%.
2. Residual RUL orifice disease — palliative; oncology to coordinate radiation planning.
3. Repeat bronchoscopy in 4–6 weeks; consider endobronchial stenting if restenosis.
4. ICU monitoring overnight.
5. Continue oncologic therapy per primary team.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOTE: note_050

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: extreme_length, conflicting_details, unicode_checkboxes

ICU BRONCHOSCOPY — COMPLEX VENTILATED PATIENT
POST-LUNG TRANSPLANT AIRWAY COMPLICATIONS EVALUATION
DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP, DAABIP
TRANSPLANT PULMONOLOGY CO-ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 56F

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSES:
  1. Bilateral lung transplantation, day 21; acute cellular rejection suspected (T86.819)
  2. New left anastomotic dehiscence, suspected on CT (J98.09)
  3. Ventilator-dependent, post-transplant (J96.00)
  4. New rise in BAL galactomannan — invasive aspergillosis vs colonization

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSES:
  1. Left anastomotic dehiscence confirmed (1.0 cm defect at LMB anastomosis — grade II per ISHLT classification)
  2. Bilateral airway mucosal changes consistent with ischemia/ischemia-reperfusion injury
  3. BAL samples obtained; galactomannan result pending
  4. No endobronchial lesion

PROCEDURE: Flexible bronchoscopy with BAL and endobronchial biopsies (CPT 31622; 31623; 31628)

ANESTHESIA: No additional sedation; patient deeply sedated on baseline propofol + fentanyl infusion; paralytic (cisatracurium) held for 30 minutes pre-procedure. [Nursing MAR documents: "cisatracurium infusion ongoing during procedure" — conflict with attending plan; MAR reviewed in real time; infusion was running at 0.1 mcg/kg/min (minimal) vs full-dose; attending confirmed acceptable level of neuromuscular recovery; MAR annotation error: infusion not stopped, titrated to minimum.]

PRE-PROCEDURE CHECKLIST (transplant bronchoscopy protocol):
  ☑ Consent: surrogate (spouse) — patient unable to consent; documented
  ☑ Time-out completed
  ☑ CMV viral load reviewed (ordered; result pending at time of procedure)
  ☑ Immunosuppression levels reviewed: tacrolimus trough 8.2 ng/mL (therapeutic)
  ☑ Coags: INR 1.5 (elevated — acceptable per transplant attending decision) 0‌
  ☑ Bronchoscopy kit: 4.9 mm scope compatible with 8.0 ETT
  ☐ Bronchial brush: not used — replaced by BAL + forceps biopsy per protocol 1‌

INSTRUMENTS: Olympus BF-P290 (4.9 mm OD); 2.0 mm cup forceps biopsy; BAL catheter.

PROCEDURE NARRATIVE:

PHASE 1 — SURVEY:
Bronchoscope introduced via 8.0 ETT. Scope passed to carina. Survey findings:

RIGHT ANASTOMOSIS (at RMB — 3 cm distal to carina):
  Anastomosis appearance: circumferential mild edema; suture line intact; no dehiscence; mucosa mildly pale/ischemic at suture margins. No granulation tissue (day 21 — early). ISHLT grade: 0 (intact anastomosis, mild ischemia).
  Right donor airways (RMB, RUL, RML, RLL): diffuse mucosal pallor and mild friability — consistent with ischemia-reperfusion; no hemorrhage; no fungal plaques.

LEFT ANASTOMOSIS (LMB — 3.5 cm distal to carina):
  Critical finding: 1.0 cm defect at posterior wall of LMB anastomosis; edges with necrotic appearance; suture line disrupted along 270-degree arc posteriorly; extraluminal tissue visible through defect. Consistent with grade II anastomotic dehiscence (ISHLT classification: partial dehiscence with necrosis but without full circumferential disruption).
  No air leak audible at this phase (cuff inflated).
  Left donor airways: mucosal pallor; LUL B1+2, LLL patent; no fungal plaques visible; mucosal friability moderate.

PHASE 2 — SAMPLING:
Transbronchial biopsies:
  Right lower lobe (B9) — 4 cup-forceps biopsies (rejection protocol; adequate cores on inspection; mild ooze, self-limited).
  Left lower lobe (B9) — 4 cup-forceps biopsies (cautious given anastomotic dehiscence; forceps did not approach anastomosis).

BAL: Right lower lobe (B9) — 60 mL instilled, 38 mL returned. Left lower lobe (B8) — 60 mL instilled, 41 mL returned.

PHASE 3 — ANASTOMOTIC ASSESSMENT AND INTERVENTION:
Left anastomotic dehiscence discussed emergently with transplant surgery. Decision: conservative management initially. Covered stent evaluated — deferred (anastomosis only day 21; stent at LMB risks occlusion of LUL orifice; surgical revision discussed). Air leak confirmed by brief cuff deflation: audible air leak on left; cuff reinflated to seal. Plan: maintain positive-pressure ventilation with cuff inflated; aggressive immunosuppression adjustment; CT surveillance.

SPECIMENS:
  A1: RLL B9 TBBx x4 — formalin; H&E (acute cellular rejection grading per Lung Rejection Study Group classification); GMS/PAS stain
  A2: LLL B9 TBBx x4 — formalin; same protocol
  B1: RLL BAL (60 mL) — galactomannan, Aspergillus PCR, CMV PCR, HSV PCR, bacterial/AFB/fungal culture, cytology, lymphocyte subsets
  B2: LLL BAL (60 mL) — same protocol

COMPLICATIONS: Mild hemorrhage at TBBX sites (both sides); controlled with cold saline. INR 1.5 — no significant coagulopathic bleeding. SpO2 maintained >92% throughout (FiO2 0.60 baseline). EBL: <10 mL.

POST-PROCEDURE PLAN:
1. Left anastomotic dehiscence grade II: emergent transplant surgery notification complete; plan for CT chest in AM; surgical revision vs covered stent to be determined at multidisciplinary conference tomorrow.
2. Maintain cuffed ventilation; do not deflate cuff until surgical plan determined.
3. Galactomannan result (BAL) expected within 24h; if >1.0, add voriconazole; current prophylaxis: micafungin.
4. Rejection protocol biopsies to pathology — result expected 48h; if A2 or greater rejection: pulse steroids.
5. CMV PCR result pending; if positive: ganciclovir.
6. Tacrolimus level rechecked in AM.
7. Transplant surgery, transplant pulmonology, and IP co-management ongoing.
8. Family updated; goals of care reviewed — aggressive treatment plan confirmed by surrogate.

NOTE: note_001

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, inconsistent_formatting

IMPRESSION / PLAN
1. Hilar adenopathy with 2.1 cm hypoechoic station 11R node, EBUS-TBNA performed; results pending.
2. Mediastinal node station 4R sampled x3 passes; station 7 sampled x2 passes.
3. Await rapid on-site cytology correlation and formal pathology; if malignant, proceed to oncology referral.
4. Continue supplemental O2 as needed; follow up in 2 weeks or upon path return.

OPERATIVE NOTE — ENDOBRONCHIAL ULTRASOUND WITH TBNA
Date: [REDACTED]
Physician: [NAME:REDACTED]
Patient: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]
Age/Sex: 67-year-old male

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, unspecified (R59.9); Right hilar mass, indeterminate (R91.8)

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same, pending pathology

PROCEDURES PERFORMED:
• Flexible bronchoscopy with endobronchial ultrasound-guided TBNA (CPT 31652)
• Rapid on-site cytologic evaluation

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation
  Midazolam 2 mg IV @ 09:14
  Fentanyl 75 mcg IV @ 09:16
  Additional fentanyl 25 mcg IV @ 09:34
  Total procedure time under sedation: 38 minutes

MONITORING: Continuous pulse oximetry, ETCO2, cardiac telemetry, noninvasive BP q5 minutes

INSTRUMENT: Olympus BF-UC180F linear EBUS bronchoscope; Vizishot 2 21-gauge TBNA needles

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
Informed consent obtained and documented. Patient positioned supine, supplemental O2 via nasal cannula maintained SpO2 >94% throughout. Topical 4% lidocaine applied to oropharynx; additional 2 mL 1% lidocaine instilled transorally at cords. EBUS bronchoscope passed transorally without resistance. Vocal cords normal. Tracheal mucosa without lesion. Carina sharp and midline.

Systematic survey performed in sequence:
  Station 4R: 1.4 cm short axis, hypoechoic with heterogeneous internal echo; 3 passes performed, each yielding visible white core material. ROSE reviewed by cytotechnologist — atypical cells noted.
  Station 7: 0.9 cm, homogeneous; 2 passes, adequate material.
  Station 11R: 2.1 cm, hypoechoic, smooth border, absence of central hilar vessel sign; 4 passes. ROSE: suspicious for malignant cells. Doppler confirmed avoidance of intervening vasculature at all stations.

Right and left mainstem bronchi inspected; mucosa unremarkable. B3 subsegmental airways patent. No endobronchial lesion identified.

Scope withdrawn atraumatically. Patient tolerated well.

SPECIMENS:
  Label A — Station 4R TBNA (x3 passes): cytology + cell block
  Label B — Station 7 TBNA (x2 passes): cytology
  Label C — Station 11R TBNA (x4 passes): cytology + cell block + molecular send-out pending path

COMPLICATIONS: None. Estimated blood loss: minimal (<1 mL).

NOTE: note_026

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, inconsistent_formatting

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN (dictated first per ICU rounding convention):
1. Percutaneous dilational tracheostomy successfully completed at bedside (ICU bay 6); Shiley 8.0 cuffed trach in satisfactory position per bronchoscopic and CXR confirmation.
2. Trach care protocol initiated; nursing education completed.
3. Ventilator settings: return to pre-procedure baseline; PEEP 8, FiO2 0.45.
4. Cuff pressure checks q8h; target 20–25 mmHg.
5. Speech-language pathology (SLP) consult placed when wean criteria met.

OPERATIVE NOTE — PERCUTANEOUS DILATIONAL TRACHEOSTOMY
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
FELLOW (supervised): [NAME:REDACTED], MD (PCCM Year 2)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 58M

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Ventilator-dependent respiratory failure (J96.00); anticipated prolonged mechanical ventilation; ARDS secondary to septic shock (J80; A41.9)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; percutaneous tracheostomy placed successfully

CPT: 31600 (tracheostomy, planned); 31622 (bronchoscopy for guidance, separate)

ANESTHESIA: Propofol bolus 80 mg IV; fentanyl 100 mcg IV; rocuronium 40 mg IV for brief paralysis. Bedside ICU procedure.

MONITORING: Continuous arterial line (right radial), SpO2, ETCO2 waveform, cardiac telemetry, noninvasive BP q2 min intraoperatively.

INSTRUMENTS:
  - Cook Ciaglia Blue Rhino percutaneous tracheostomy kit (8.0 mm)
  - Olympus BF-P290 flexible bronchoscope (4.9 mm OD), introduced via ETT
  - Shiley 8.0 cuffed tracheostomy tube with inner cannula

CLINICAL SUMMARY:
58-year-old male, ICU day 12 for ARDS complicating gram-negative septic shock. Intubated via orotracheal route (7.5 ETT, depth 23 cm at lip). Sedation weaning attempted; agitation and ventilator dyssynchrony precluding liberation trial. Multidisciplinary team recommendation: tracheostomy for prolonged ventilator management. Contraindications assessed: neck circumference 42 cm (large habitus, noted); cervical spine clearance confirmed. No prior neck surgery. Platelet 156k, INR 1.1.

PROCEDURE NARRATIVE:
Patient placed supine, neck extended over shoulder roll. Landmarks: thyroid cartilage, cricoid, tracheal rings palpated. Target interspaced between rings 2–3 confirmed by direct palpation and bronchoscopic illumination through the anterior tracheal wall. ETT withdrawn to 18 cm at lip under bronchoscopic guidance (carina confirmed at safe distance of 7 cm above planned entry).

Skin prepared and draped in sterile fashion. Vertical 1.5 cm skin incision at ring 2–3 interspace. Blunt dissection through subcutaneous tissue to pretracheal fascia. 14-gauge introducer needle inserted under bronchoscopic visualization: needle tip visualized in tracheal lumen between rings 2–3, confirming midline entry. 0.038-inch J-tip guidewire advanced; position confirmed bronchoscopically (wire coiling distally in trachea). 14 Fr fascial dilator followed by Blue Rhino single-step dilator sequentially advanced over wire under bronchoscopic surveillance; airway lumen maintained throughout — SpO2 nadir 94%.

Shiley 8.0 cuffed trach tube loaded on obturator, advanced over guidewire and dilator peel-away, seated in trachea. Obturator removed; inner cannula inserted. Ventilator circuit connected; bilateral breath sounds confirmed; ETCO2 waveform present. ETT removed. Cuff inflated to 22 mmHg (cuff manometer).

Bronchoscopic check through trach tube: tube tip 4 cm above carina; midline; no posterior wall injury; no active hemorrhage. Tracheal mucosa at entry site: intact circumferentially.

Trach tube secured with Velcro trach holder + suture (2-0 silk x2 to flange).

SPECIMENS: None.

COMPLICATIONS: None. Transient SpO2 drop to 94% during dilation (resolved with FiO2 increase to 1.0 for 90 seconds). Estimated blood loss: <5 mL.

NOTE: note_032

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: inconsistent_formatting, copy_paste_artifacts

TRACHEOSTOMY EXCHANGE NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 62M

INDICATION: Mature tracheostomy (14 days post-PDT); first trach tube exchange for smaller cuffless tube per ventilator-weaning protocol. Current tube: Shiley 8.0 cuffed (original PDT tube).

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Tracheostomy in situ, mature tract (Z93.0)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; tube exchanged to Shiley 6.0 cuffless

PROCEDURE: Tracheostomy tube change, mature tract. Bedside ICU.

ANESTHESIA: No sedation required — patient alert and cooperative. Supplemental O2 via trach mask during exchange.

TECHNIQUE:
Bedside setup: new Shiley 6.0 cuffless tube with obturator and inner cannula; 10 Fr suction catheter loaded as guide; Ambu bag and 100% O2 source at bedside. Tracheal dilators available.

Existing tube: cuff deflated under suction (12 mL air withdrawn). Patient instructed to breathe slowly. Old tube removed in one controlled motion. 6.0 cuffless Shiley immediately advanced through mature stoma tract over 10 Fr suction catheter guide without resistance. Inner cannula inserted. Trach mask O2 reconnected immediately. SpO2 stable throughout (98% → 97% → 99%).

Position confirmed: bronchoscope introduced through 6.0 cuffless tube; tip 4.5 cm above carina; no false passage; mucosa at stoma and subglottic space intact. 

patientpatient [sic] reported mild discomfort during cuff deflation — corrected: patient reported mild discomfort which resolved after exchange was complete.

Tube secured with new Velcro holder.

COMPLICATIONS: None. Desaturation not observed. EBL: None.

PLAN:
1. PMV (Passy-Muir valve) trial this afternoon per SLP order.
2. Capping trials per weaning schedule.
3. Decannulation assessment in 1 week.

NOTE: note_034
