NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: malformed_codes, inconsistent_formatting

TRACHEOSTOMY TUBE EXCHANGE — DIFFICULT (IMMATURE TRACT)
DATE: [REDACTED]   ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 39F
INSTITUTION: [NAME:REDACTED] MICU

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Tracheostomy in situ, day 6 (early/immature tract); accidental decannulation overnight; nursing requested urgent tube reinsertion (J95.04)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; trach tube reinserted, position confirmed

PROCEDURES / CPT:
  31502    — Tracheostomy tube change (immature tract) [billing note: 31502:U1 vs 31502-U1 per payer format — modifier for immature tract designation, confirm with coder]
  31622    — Flexible bronchoscopy for confirmation

ANESTHESIA: Midazolam 1 mg IV (anxiolysis only); local 1% lidocaine 2 mL at stoma margins topically.

CLINICAL NOTE:
39-year-old female, day 6 post-PDT (performed for respiratory failure complicating status asthmaticus). Overnight nursing note: patient self-decannulated at approximately 0300. Nurse re-attempted bedside insertion with 7.5 Shiley — resistance encountered, tube not inserted; patient placed on bag-valve-mask via stoma orifice (adequate ventilation maintained per nursing note). IP fellow called STAT; attending arrived with kit.

PROCEDURE:
Stoma examined: tract partially closed (day 6 — immature, not yet epithelialized). Hemostats passed gently through stoma to re-dilate tract. 5.0 Shiley cuffed (smaller than prior 7.5) advanced through stoma with obturator under direct bronchoscopic visualization — scope introduced simultaneously via ETT (orotracheal ETT placed emergently by anesthesia team as backup airway prior to IP arrival; cords visualized, 7.0 ETT in trachea). Trach stoma approached from anterior; scope light visible through stoma confirmed intratracheal position; 5.0 Shiley guided into lumen without false passage.

Cuff inflated. Patient ventilated via trach. Orotracheal ETT removed.

Bronchoscopy through Shiley: tip 5 cm above carina; posterior wall intact; no false passage confirmed.

Tube exchanged 30 min later (after patient stabilized): 7.0 Shiley inserted over Aintree catheter (airway exchange catheter) under bronchoscopic guidance; position re-confirmed.

COMPLICATIONS: Accidental decannulation event (nursing occurrence report filed). False passage not created. Hypoxemia during event (SpO2 nadir 81% per nursing, resolved with BVM ventilation pre-IP arrival). EBL: None.

PLAN:
1. Trach holder changed to suture (2-0 silk x2); strict "no trach tie removal without physician at bedside" order.
2. Daily neck and stoma inspection.
3. Incident review per quality process.

NOTE: note_049

NOTE TEXT:

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, inconsistent_formatting, table_with_tabs

ICU BRONCHOSCOPY — HEMOPTYSIS EVALUATION IN VENTILATED PATIENT
DATE: [REDACTED]   ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP
FELLOW: [NAME:REDACTED], MD (PCCM Year 1, supervised)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 71M

PRELIMINARY FINDINGS AND CLINICAL IMPRESSION (per MICU rounding handoff, documented first):
Active endobronchial hemorrhage localized to right upper lobe B2 (posterior segment); no endobronchial mass identified; clot extracted; hemorrhage controlled with topical epinephrine; etiology unclear (consider ulcerated pulmonary artery catheter injury vs aspergillus angioinvasion given profound neutropenia); bronchoscopy tolerated; additional workup as below.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Hemoptysis in ventilated patient; MICU day 5; AML induction chemotherapy (profound neutropenia, ANC 0.0) (D70.1); suspected invasive pulmonary aspergillosis (IPA) vs pulmonary artery catheter-related injury (R04.2)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: RUL B2 active hemorrhage; clot; etiology pending (PA catheter wedge position in B2 confirmed on CXR retrospectively)

PROCEDURE: Flexible bronchoscopy, diagnostic and therapeutic (CPT 31622; 31623)

ANESTHESIA: Propofol 40 mg bolus + fentanyl 50 mcg bolus (addition to baseline ICU infusions). No paralytic.

BRONCHOSCOPIC FINDINGS:

	Location	Appearance	Action Taken
	Trachea	Blood-streaked secretions throughout	Suctioned
	Carina	Sharp; blood emanating from RMB	—
	RUL B2 (posterior)	Active ooze; 1.2 cm adherent clot	Clot extracted with suction; epinephrine instilled
	RUL B1, B3	Patent; mild blood staining	Suctioned
	RLL, RML	Patent; blood-stained secretions	BAL obtained RML
	Left-sided airways	Clear; no blood	—

Epinephrine 1:10,000 solution, 3 mL, instilled at RUL B2 site; hemorrhage reduced to minimal ooze. Cold saline lavage 10 mL x2.

BAL (RML B4) — sent for:
  Galactomannan (BAL galactomannan threshold >1.0 for IPA)
  Beta-D-glucan, Aspergillus PCR
  Bacterial/AFB/fungal culture
  Cytology

PA catheter position reviewed on CXR post-bronchoscopy: tip confirmed in RUL B2 position — balloon likely over-wedged. PA catheter withdrawn 3 cm by ICU team; tip repositioned to main PA.

SPECIMENS: RML BAL — galactomannan, culture, cytology; RUL B2 clot — sent in saline to pathology (fungal hyphae/GMS stain).

COMPLICATIONS: None new. Transient SpO2 drop 91% during clot extraction; recovered with FiO2 increase. EBL: 10–15 mL total suctioned.

PLAN:
1. PA catheter repositioned — monitor for recurrent hemoptysis.
2. Serum galactomannan + BAL galactomannan; if positive: escalate to voriconazole (currently on prophylactic micafungin).
3. CT chest (high-res) if patient stable enough — halo sign, angioinvasion pattern.
4. Interventional radiology bronchial artery embolization on standby if hemorrhage recurs and patient survives to transportation.
5. Infectious disease co-management.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: noisy_units, conflicting_details

MONTGOMERY T-TUBE INSERTION — IDIOPATHIC SUBGLOTTIC STENOSIS
OPERATIVE NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP
CO-SURGEON: [NAME:REDACTED], MD (Laryngology / ENT)
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 34F

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Idiopathic subglottic stenosis (iSGS), recurrent, grade III; failed serial balloon dilation x4 (J38.6)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; T-tube inserted; phonation preserved

PROCEDURE: Rigid bronchoscopy, Nd:YAG laser, balloon dilation, Montgomery T-tube insertion

ANESTHESIA: GA, TIVA, spontaneous. [Nursing intake documents "supraglottic airway" — confirmed with anesthesia: LMA Fastrach #4 placed initially; removed when rigid bronchoscope passed transorally, per standard T-tube procedure; notation in nursing form is partially incorrect — rigid bronchoscope served as ventilation device, not LMA throughout the operative phase. Anesthesia record addendum pending.]

MEDICATION LOG:
  Propofol infusion: 100–130 mcg/kg/min
  Remifentanil: 0.08 mcg/kg/min
  Topical lidocaine 4%, 3m mL [noisy — intended 3 mL] via rigid scope

PROCEDURE:
Rigid bronchoscope (8.5 mm → 10.5 mm). Subglottic web: grade III, fibrous, 1.0 cm below cords, 2.8 cm length, 3 mm residual lumen. Nd:YAG laser: radial incisions x4 (15 W, 0.3 sec pulses, contact mode, ~35 pulses). Serial dilation: 10 → 12 mm CRE balloon. Post-dilation lumen 12 mm. Stoma (ring 3, 2 cm below cricoid): 1.5 cm incision. T-tube selected: 11 mm × 60 mm × 25 mm sidearm (smaller tube given female anatomy and smaller tracheal diameter). T-tube folded, introduced via rigid barrel; upper limb maneuvered above cords (laryngoscope view confirmed — no arytenoid contact); sidearm exteriorized through stoma. Flexible scope via sidearm: lumen patent; upper limb visible above cords; distal end 4 cm above carina. Phonation with sidearm plugged: good vocal quality.

COMPLICATIONS: SpO2 dip to 86% during proximal limb seating — self-limited; ventilation via rigid scope maintained; SpO2 recovered to 97% within 2 minutes. EBL: ~10 mL. Grade I mucosal lacerations at laser sites.

PLAN: T-tube care protocol. SLP. IP/ENT co-follow-up 2 weeks. Autoimmune workup (GPA, anti-PR3) to confirm idiopathic vs vasculitic etiology — ordered.

MONTGOMERY T-TUBE MANAGEMENT — TUBE EXCHANGE (ROUTINE)
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD   PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE: 67M

INDICATION: Routine T-tube exchange; T-tube in situ 14 months; encrustation and partial inner surface obstruction on prior bronchoscopy.

PRE-PROCEDURE CHECKLIST:
  ☑ Consent obtained
  ☑ ENT co-present for upper limb manipulation
  ☑ New T-tube (same size: 14 mm × 80 mm × 35 mm) confirmed at bedside
  ☑ Rigid bronchoscope and laser on standby 0‌
  ☑ GA/anesthesia team present 1‌
  ☐ Tracheoplasty not planned — ENT evaluation completed, no structural change

PROCEDURE:
GA induced. Rigid bronchoscope introduced through existing stoma. T-tube identified; tracheal limb patent but inner surface with thick mucoid encrustation. Sidearm sutures released. Flexible bronchoscope passed via sidearm — granulation tissue at proximal stoma margin (3 × 4 mm); trimmed with forceps/cold instrument; sent to pathology (r/o malignant transformation). Old T-tube removed via rigid barrel after folding with T-tube forceps — sidearm withdrawn through stoma simultaneously with assistant coordination.

Stoma and tracheal lumen inspected: mucosa pink, no significant injury at old tube site. New T-tube (14 × 80 × 35 mm) folded and inserted via rigid barrel; repositioned identically; sidearm exteriorized. Position confirmed.

POST-PROCEDURE CHECKLIST:
  ☑ Flexible bronchoscopy via sidearm: lumen patent; upper limb 1.5 cm above cords; distal end 3 cm above carina
  ☑ Phonation confirmed (sidearm plugged)
  ☑ New sutures placed; dressing applied

SPECIMENS: Stoma granulation tissue — formalin; H&E (r/o recurrent ACC or squamous metaplasia).
COMPLICATIONS: None. EBL: <5 mL.

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: table_with_tabs, conflicting_details

PROCEDURE NOTE — PDT COMPLICATED BY POSTERIOR TRACHEAL WALL INJURY
DATE: [REDACTED]   ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 74M

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Prolonged ventilator dependence; ICU day 21; neuromuscular weakness (J96.00)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Percutaneous tracheostomy; posterior tracheal wall laceration (1.5 cm) — managed conservatively; trach in satisfactory position

PROCEDURE: PDT (Ciaglia Blue Rhino); CPT 31600; 31622

ANESTHESIA: Propofol infusion; fentanyl 100 mcg; vecuronium 6 mg.

PROCEDURE:
Blue Rhino PDT performed. At time of Blue Rhino dilator advancement (over guidewire), slight resistance noted; maintained bronchoscopic visualization — dilator tip briefly deflected posteriorly; small (1.5 cm) posterior tracheal wall laceration identified on immediate post-insertion bronchoscopy.

COMPLICATION MANAGEMENT:

	Finding	Description	Management
	Posterior wall tear	1.5 cm linear; mucosa only; no full-thickness (muscularis intact on bronchoscopy)	Conservative; tube positioned distal to tear
	Hemorrhage	Minimal ooze (<5 mL) from laceration	Cold saline; self-limited
	Trach position	Tip 5 cm above carina; away from laceration	Satisfactory

Thoracic surgery consulted urgently — reviewed bronchoscopy images; recommended conservative management given superficial laceration without pneumomediastinum or tension. CT chest: no pneumomediastinum; no subcutaneous emphysema. Laceration monitored with repeat flexible bronchoscopy at 24h, 72h, 1 week.

SPECIMENS: None.

COMPLICATIONS: Posterior tracheal wall mucosa laceration (1.5 cm, superficial); managed conservatively. EBL: <5 mL. Thoracic surgery notified and in agreement with plan. [Note: prior nursing pre-procedure note documents "difficult anatomy noted" — attending was not informed pre-procedure; review of note timing confirms nurse note added to chart after procedure, not before — documentation timeline under review.]

PLAN:
1. Repeat bronchoscopy 24h, 72h, 1 week to confirm laceration healing.
2. CT chest without contrast to exclude pneumomediastinum — obtained, negative.
3. Keep trach cuff inflated to protect laceration from aspiration.
4. Thoracic surgery co-management.
5. Adverse event report filed.

PDT PROCEDURE CHECKLIST AND NOTE
FACILITY: [NAME:REDACTED] MICU   DATE: [REDACTED]

  ☑ Emergency surgical airway kit: at bedside 1‌

  ☑ CXR ordered
  ☑ Ventilator reconnected; ETCO2 confirmed
  ☑ Dressing applied; flange secured

COMPLICATIONS: None. EBL: <5 mL. SpO2 maintained >93% throughout.

================================================================================
NOTE: note_025
================================================================================

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: extreme_length, table_with_tabs, reordered_sections

OPERATIVE REPORT — COMPLEX THERAPEUTIC BRONCHOSCOPY
RIGID BRONCHOSCOPY + STENT PLACEMENT + BALLOON DILATION
TRACHEOESOPHAGEAL FISTULA — COVERED STENT MANAGEMENT
DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP
FELLOW OBSERVER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 69M
INSTITUTION: [NAME:REDACTED] Academic Medical Center

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN (per operative note header convention):
1. Large acquired TEF (tracheoesophageal fistula), mid-tracheal, post-esophagectomy radiation — covered SEMS deployed; esophageal stent placement to follow by GI endoscopy (separate procedure, same day).
2. Airway patency restored; fistula orifice sealed on airway side.
3. ICU admission post-procedure.
4. Pulmonary hygiene, enteral nutrition via J-tube.
5. Follow-up flexible bronchoscopy 4 weeks.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS:
  1. Acquired tracheoesophageal fistula (TEF), mid-tracheal, post-esophagectomy, post-radiation (J86.0)
  2. Recurrent aspiration pneumonia from TEF (J69.0)
  3. Prior esophageal stent (placed externally, now malpositioned — GI to address)

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; covered fully deployed tracheal SEMS placed across TEF; tracheal lumen patent; fistula orifice covered.

PROCEDURES:
  31631     — Rigid bronchoscopy with stent placement (covered SEMS)
  31641     — Therapeutic bronchoscopy (APC, granulation tissue prior to stent)
  31630     — Balloon bronchoplasty (pre-stent dilation)

ANESTHESIA: GA; TIVA; jet ventilation via Karl Storz 12.5 mm rigid bronchoscope. Anesthesiologist: [NAME:REDACTED], MD. MAC level ≥ 0.8; neuromuscular blockade for jet ventilation.

CLINICAL BACKGROUND:
69-year-old male with esophageal squamous cell carcinoma treated with neoadjuvant chemoradiation followed by esophagectomy (Ivor-Lewis, 14 months prior). Post-operative course complicated by anastomotic leak (managed conservatively). Now presenting with new-onset persistent cough with oral intake, recurrent aspiration pneumonia (3 episodes in 2 months), weight loss 12 lbs, and dyspnea. CT chest/neck: fistulous track at mid-tracheal level (approximately 4 cm below subglottis), 1.8 cm in diameter, communicating with esophagus/neo-esophagus. Tracheal narrowing 50% at fistula site due to granulation tissue proliferation. Malpositioned esophageal stent noted (proximal flange above fistula, covering insufficiently). Multidisciplinary conference involving thoracic surgery, GI, radiation oncology, interventional pulmonology: plan for combined airway + esophageal stenting same day.

PROCEDURE NARRATIVE:

PHASE 1 — DIAGNOSTIC RIGID BRONCHOSCOPY:
Rigid bronchoscope introduced under GA + jet ventilation. Subglottic space: patent. Tracheal lumen: mid-tracheal level, gross fistulous orifice visualized on posterior-right lateral wall; 1.5–2.0 cm in diameter; granulation tissue fronds surrounding margins; active secretion egress from esophageal side visible during jet ventilation cycles. Mucosa proximal and distal to fistula: mild erythema, no tumor recurrence visualized. Distal trachea and carina: patent. Both mainstem bronchi: patent. No endobronchial tumor.

PHASE 2 — GRANULATION TISSUE ABLATION:
Flexible bronchoscope introduced through rigid barrel. APC applied to granulation tissue surrounding fistula margins (1.5 L/min flow, 50 W; approximately 60 pulses); tissue ablation and debulking achieved. Mucosal hemostasis confirmed. Fistula margins now clearly delineated: 1.5 cm oval defect on posterior tracheal wall.

PHASE 3 — BALLOON DILATION PRE-STENT:
Tracheal stenosis at fistula site (50% narrowing due to granuloma) dilated with 16 mm CRE balloon to 8 atm x 60 seconds. Lumen expanded to approximately 14 mm at narrowest. Mucosa laceration grade I at posterior wall (acceptable, expected). Cold saline irrigation for hemostasis.

PHASE 4 — COVERED SEMS DEPLOYMENT:
Stent selection and sizing (see table):

	Stent Characteristic	Specification
	Type	Fully covered SEMS (Micro-Tech Niti-S)
	Diameter	18 mm
	Length	80 mm
	Coverage	Full silicone cover; proximal flange 22 mm
	Target position	Mid-trachea, 3 cm below subglottis to 3 cm above carina

Covered SEMS deployed via rigid delivery system under direct bronchoscopic and fluoroscopic guidance. Stent released; proximal flange positioned under laryngoscope visualization. Post-deployment inspection via flexible scope through rigid barrel:
  - Stent proximal end: 3 cm below vocal cords, flange apposed to tracheal wall.
  - Fistula site: covered by stent wall; no residual air/secretion egress from esophageal side visible.
  - Stent distal end: 3 cm above carina; bilateral orifices patent.
  - Stent lumen: patent, round, no collapse; estimated tracheal patency >90%.

PHASE 5 — FLEXIBLE BRONCHOSCOPY FINAL SURVEY:
Flexible scope passed through stent lumen: bilateral mainstem and lobar bronchi confirmed patent. Secretions cleared. Carina visible and sharp below stent.

Procedure ended; jet ventilation discontinued; patient transferred to ICU intubated for observation.

COORDINATION WITH GI:
GI endoscopy team on standby; esophageal stent revision performed immediately after airway procedure in same OR suite. Airway bronchoscopist available for re-entry if airway compromise during esophageal procedure. No complications during esophageal phase per GI colleague's report.

SPECIMENS:
  Granulation tissue from fistula margins — APC-ablated fragments (small, cauterized) sent for histology to rule out recurrent SCC.

POST-PROCEDURE NOTES:
	Parameter	Pre-procedure	Post-procedure
	Tracheal patency	~50%	>90%
	TEF orifice coverage	Open	Covered (SEMS)
	SpO2 on FiO2 0.4	91%	97%
	Peak airway pressure	44 cm H2O	26 cm H2O

COMPLICATIONS: Grade I tracheal mucosal laceration (expected; hemostasis achieved). Transient hypoxemia during granuloma ablation (SpO2 84% for ~45 seconds; recovered with brief pause and FiO2 increase). No airway fire. No stent migration intraoperatively. EBL: <10 mL.

IMPRESSION/PLAN:
1. Acquired TEF successfully managed with covered tracheal SEMS; airway side of fistula sealed.
2. Esophageal stent revised by GI (separate operative report).
3. ICU monitoring; enteral nutrition via J-tube only; strict NPO by mouth.
4. Daily chest assessment; bronchoscopy re-entry at 4 weeks for stent check.
5. Radiation oncology review re: reirradiation feasibility deferred — airway stabilization first priority.
6. Palliative care consultation for goals of care discussion given disease burden.

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY NOTE
PROCEDURE: EBUS-TBNA
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 49M

PRELIMINARY RESULTS / ROSE FINDINGS (documented first per ICU handoff protocol):
  - Station 7: malignant cells, large cell morphology. ROSE performed by cytotechnologist. Cell block and molecular hold placed.
  - Station 4R: insufficient sample on first pass; 3 additional passes performed; final ROSE: atypical cells.
  - Station 11L: benign lymphocytes.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Newly detected hilar and mediastinal adenopathy; PET-avid nodal disease; no known primary (R59.9)

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Station 7: malignant neoplasm of mediastinum, histology pending (pending C38.3 vs metastatic)

PROCEDURE: Linear EBUS-TBNA (CPT 31652)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation. Midazolam 4 mg + fentanyl 100 mcg IV. Topical 4% lidocaine 6 mL total.

PROCEDURE NARRATIVE:
Informed consent reviewed and confirmed. Time-out completed. EBUS bronchoscope (BF-UC180F) introduced transorally. Trachea patent, carina midline without widening. Survey performed:

  Station 2R: Node 0.6 cm — not sampled (below threshold).
  Station 4R: Node 1.4 cm, hypoechoic — 4 passes total (1st pass insufficient per ROSE; 3 additional). Final: atypical cells.
  Station 7: Node 2.8 cm — 4 passes; ROSE: malignant.
  Station 11L: Node 1.0 cm — 2 passes; benign lymphocytes.
  Station 4L: Not identified at this session.

Bilateral mainstem and lobar bronchi inspected — no endobronchial lesion.

SPECIMENS:
  Label 1 — Station 4R, 4 passes: cytology + cell block
  Label 2 — Station 7, 4 passes: cytology + cell block + molecular hold (NGS, PD-L1)
  Label 3 — Station 11L, 2 passes: cytology

COMPLICATIONS: None. EBL: <1 mL.

PLAN:
1. Formal pathology 5–7 days.
2. PET/CT body map if not already done — order placed.
3. If large cell carcinoma confirmed: hematology/oncology referral; consider immunotherapy.
4. Pulmonary hypertension workup deferred pending oncology priorities.

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: missing_section, unicode_checkboxes, inconsistent_formatting

PLEUROSCOPY AND BIOPSY PROCEDURE NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 55F

INDICATION: Undiagnosed left-sided exudative effusion, recurrent x3; cytology x2 negative; suspected malignancy (J91.8)

DIAGNOSES: Pre: Exudative pleural effusion, unspecified etiology. Post: Parietal pleural nodularity; biopsies obtained; pending.

☑ Consent obtained and documented
☑ Pre-procedure US: large L effusion, anechoic, 5.1 cm depth at entry point 0‌
☑ Time-out complete
☐ EBUS-TBNA (not performed — no mediastinal adenopathy on CT)
☑ Lab review: INR 1.0, Plt 310k, BMP within normal limits

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation + intercostal block (lidocaine 2% with epinephrine, 3 mL per space, levels 5/6/7 on left)
  Midazolam 3 mg; fentanyl 125 mcg in divided doses

PROCEDURE IN DETAIL:
Patient right lateral decubitus (L side up). Entry: 7th ICS, mid-axillary line, left. Skin incision 1.5 cm; blunt digital dissection; pleural space entered; semi-rigid thoracoscope introduced (Olympus LTF-160). Effusion (approximately 600 mL pale yellow fluid) drained to improve visualization.

Pleural findings:
  Parietal: multiple 2–6 mm tan-white nodules, particularly along the posterior costal surface and mediastinal pleura; diaphragmatic surface with flat plaques.
  Visceral: mild surface irregularity, no discrete nodules.
  No obvious gross tumor burden; pattern suspicious for diffuse malignant process.

Six biopsies taken from parietal nodules and diaphragmatic plaques using cup forceps. All specimens placed in formalin.

1‌ IHC panel requested: calretinin, CK5/6, WT-1, D2-40, TTF-1, Napsin A, CEA, BAP1 (BAP1 loss suggestive of mesothelioma).

Pleurodesis deferred pending pathology — if malignant, will discuss with patient.

Port closed with interrupted 2-0 Vicryl sutures. Sterile dressing.

SPECIMENS:
  Pleural fluid: LDH, protein, cytology, culture
  Biopsy x6: parietal and diaphragmatic nodules — formalin; comprehensive IHC panel

COMPLICATIONS: Vasovagal response at scope insertion; atropine 0.5 mg IV given; HR improved from 42 to 68 bpm within 2 minutes. Procedure continued without further complication. EBL: <5 mL.

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: malformed_codes, copy_paste_artifacts

PROCEDURE NOTE — AIRWAY STENT REMOVAL AND REPLACEMENT
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 74F
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Silicone tracheal stent in situ (placed 6 months prior for post-radiation tracheomalacia); recurrent mucus plugging; dyspnea (J39.8)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; stent repositioned and cleaned; airway patency confirmed

PROCEDURES:
  31631     — Rigid bronchoscopy with stent manipulation/repositioning
  31622-51  — Flexible bronchoscopy [modifier 51: multiple procedures, same session; verify 31622:51 per payer]
  31640     — Bronchoscopy w/ excision of tumor [if applicable — granulation tissue only; code under review]

ANESTHESIA: GA; jet ventilation via rigid barrel.

CLINICAL BACKGROUND: 74-year-old 74-year-old female [sic] with history of tracheal adenoid cystic carcinoma treated with resection and adjuvant radiation. Post-radiation tracheomalacia with Dumon stent in place (18 mm x 60 mm). Presenting with dyspnea and recurrent respiratory infections; chest CT showed mucus accumulation proximal to stent, stent migration distally by approximately 8–10 mm.

PROCEDURE:
Rigid bronchoscope (12.5 mm) introduced under GA + jet ventilation. Tracheal stent identified: migrated distally, tip 8 mm from carina (original plan: 15 mm from carina). Mucus impaction: thick tenacious plugging of distal stent lumen and right limb.

Stent removed with forceps through rigid barrel. Mucus cleared with lavage and suction. Granulation tissue at proximal edge of stent contact (posterior wall) — trimmed with electrocautery snare (minimal, 3 × 2 mm tissue). Flexible bronchoscope passed through rigid barrel for distal airway inspection: bilateral mainstem and lobar bronchi clear after aggressive lavage.

Repeat stent selection: same 18 mm × 60 mm Dumon stent repositioned (prior stent intact, re-used per IP attending decision). Deployed with stent pusher; repositioned to 15 mm from carina under direct rigid vision. Post-placement flexible check: stent well-seated; bilateral orifices patent.

patientpatient [sic] recovered in PACU without incident.

SPECIMENS: Granulation tissue from proximal stent contact site — formalin (rule out recurrent ACC).

COMPLICATIONS: None. Mild hemorrhage from granuloma resection; self-limited. EBL: <5 mL.

PLAN:
1. Saline nebulization QID; chest physiotherapy.
2. Stent surveillance bronchoscopy in 3 months.
3. Radiation oncology surveillance per existing schedule.
4. Pulmonary hygiene education reinforced.

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: conflicting_details, table_with_tabs, noisy_units

OPERATIVE REPORT
PERCUTANEOUS DILATIONAL TRACHEOSTOMY WITH BRONCHOSCOPIC GUIDANCE
DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, FCCP, DAABIP
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   DOB: [DOB:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 64M

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Ventilator-dependent respiratory failure, prolonged (J96.00); guillain-barré syndrome ascending paralysis (G61.0); failed extubation x2
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; Shiley 8.0 cuffed tracheostomy tube in place, confirmed

PROCEDURES: CPT 31600; 31622 (bronchoscopic guidance)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation [CONFLICT: anesthesia record documents "bedside general anesthesia with propofol infusion"; OR intake form lists "moderate sedation" — confirmed via medication MAR: propofol 150 mcg/kg/min infusion + fentanyl 10 0 mcg [noisy—intended 100 mcg] IV bolus; paralytic: cisatracurium 15 mg IV; bedside ICU procedure, no anesthesiologist present; attending performed own sedation per ICU credentialing].

PRE-PROCEDURE ASSESSMENT:

	Parameter	Value	Acceptable?
	INR	1.3	Yes
	Platelets	89k	Marginal — discussed, proceeded with caution
	Neck circumference	38 cm	Moderate habitus
	ETT depth	22 cm at lip (7.5 ETT)	Confirmed
	PEEP	10 cm H2O	Pre-procedure
	FiO2	0.55	Pre-procedure SpO2 95%

PROCEDURE:
Patient supine, neck extended. Landmarks: thyroid notch, cricoid, tracheal rings. ETT withdrawn to 17 cm under bronchoscopic guidance; carina identified 8 cm distal to planned entry (ring 2–3 interspace). 14-gauge introducer needle entered skin vertically at ring 2–3; bronchoscope confirmed midline entry and needle tip position in tracheal lumen. J-tip guidewire advanced. Fascial dilator, then Blue Rhino single-step dilator deployed under continuous bronchoscopic surveillance — tracheal back wall never endangered. Peel-away sheath placed; Shiley 8.0 loaded on obturator and advanced. Tube seated, cuff inflated to 21 mmHg.

Post-insertion bronchoscopy: tube tip 4.5 cm above carina; posterior tracheal wall intact; mild ooze at entry site, controlled with direct pressure.

Platelets: 1 unit random-donor platelets transfused intraoperatively (due to Plt 89k and ooze at site).

SPECIMENS: None.

COMPLICATIONS: Minor procedural hemorrhage at trach entry site (platelet-related); controlled with compression and platelet transfusion. EBL: approximately 15–20 mL. No pneumothorax. No posterior tracheal wall perforation.

PLAN:
1. Trach site: dry dressing; daily site care per protocol.
2. Hematology notified re: thrombocytopenia (suspected GBS-related; also on IVIG).
3. PMV (Passy-Muir valve) candidacy reassess as GBS improves.
4. Repeat bedside bronchoscopy in 48h if any concerns.

NOTE: note_006

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: missing_section, extreme_length

INTERVENTIONAL PULMONOLOGY OPERATIVE NOTE
PROCEDURE DATE: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
INSTITUTION: [NAME:REDACTED]
PATIENT: [PATIENT]    MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]    AGE/SEX: 63M

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS:
  1. Right-sided exudative pleural effusion, recurrent (J91.8)
  2. Suspected malignant pleural mesothelioma vs metastatic disease, R/O primary

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS:
  1. Right-sided complex exudative effusion with loculation (J90)
  2. Pleural biopsy specimens obtained; pathology pending
  3. Talc slurry pleurodesis performed

PROCEDURES:
  1. Diagnostic thoracentesis with ultrasound guidance (CPT 32555)
  2. Medical thoracoscopy (pleuroscopy) under moderate sedation (CPT 32601)
  3. Pleural biopsy x6 (CPT 32609)
  4. Talc slurry pleurodesis (CPT 32560)
  5. Small-bore chest tube placement, 14 Fr (CPT 32557)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation. Midazolam 3 mg IV; fentanyl 150 mcg IV in divided doses; additional midazolam 1 mg at 30 min. Local anesthetic: lidocaine 1% with epinephrine 1:100,000, approximately 15 mL total infiltrated at port sites.

POSITIONING AND SETUP:
Patient positioned in left lateral decubitus, right side up. Ultrasound (Sonosite Edge II, 6–13 MHz linear probe) performed pre-procedurally: confirmed large right-sided effusion, estimated volume 850–1000 mL, with internal echogenicity and fibrin stranding noted in the dependent portion. No significant lung sliding in the right base. Entry site marked in the 6th intercostal space, mid-axillary line (MAL). Skin prepared and draped in sterile fashion. Local anesthetic infiltrated in layered fashion to pleura (confirmed by aspiration of pleural fluid prior to incision).

THORACENTESIS:
Initial diagnostic aspirate obtained: 150 mL of amber-yellow, turbid fluid. Sent for: LDH, protein, glucose, pH, cytology, AFB/fungal/bacterial culture, CEA, mesothelin. Pleural/serum LDH ratio and protein ratio to follow for Light's criteria categorization.

THORACOSCOPY:
Following initial aspirate and partial drainage, a 1.5 cm skin incision made with 15-blade scalpel at the marked site. Blunt dissection through intercostal musculature to pleural space. A 9 mm semi-rigid thoracoscope (Olympus LTF-160) inserted after digital confirmation of pleural space entry. Remaining effusion drained under direct visualization.

Pleural survey:
  - Visceral pleura: diffuse nodular thickening with multiple white plaques, 2–8 mm, studding the right lower and mid parietal pleura; visceral pleura irregular with fibrin deposits.
  - Parietal pleura: nodular deposits along posterior chest wall and diaphragm; surface appears grossly involved.
  - Diaphragmatic pleura: papillary projections, 3–5 mm; highly suspicious for malignant involvement.
  - No clear mass lesion; pattern consistent with diffuse pleural carcinomatosis vs mesothelioma.
  - Loculation present in the posterior inferior compartment; blunt breakdown of loculation performed under direct thoracoscopic visualization.

Biopsies: Six biopsies obtained using large-cup thoracoscopic forceps (3 from parietal plaques, 2 from diaphragmatic projections, 1 from visceral pleural nodule). Adequate tissue volume confirmed by visual inspection.

PLEURODESIS:
Following biopsy, talc slurry prepared: 4 g sterile-graded talc (Novatech) mixed with 100 mL 0.9% NaCl; slurry instilled via thoracoscope under direct vision. Distribution confirmed visually throughout accessible pleural space. Port site temporarily sealed; patient repositioned from side to side x4 to facilitate distribution.

CHEST TUBE:
14 Fr small-bore pleural catheter (Pleuracan) placed through the thoracoscope port under direct vision and secured with 0-silk suture and adhesive dressing. Connected to water-seal drainage at -20 cm H2O.

POST-PROCEDURE MANAGEMENT:
Patient transferred to recovery. CXR obtained: moderate residual right-sided effusion, tube in satisfactory position. SpO2 98% on 2 L NC. No pneumothorax on contralateral side.

SPECIMENS:
  Tube 1: Pleural fluid (150 mL) — chemistry, cytology, cultures, biomarkers
  Jar A: Parietal pleural biopsy x3 — formalin, H&E, IHC panel (CK5/6, WT-1, calretinin, D2-40, TTF-1, Napsin A, CEA)
  Jar B: Diaphragmatic biopsy x2 — formalin
  Jar C: Visceral pleural biopsy x1 — formalin + additional tissue in saline for potential flow cytometry

COMPLICATIONS: None significant. Mild vasovagal response at start of procedure; resolved with brief Trendelenburg position and fluid bolus (250 mL NS). Estimated blood loss: <5 mL.

IMPRESSION/PLAN:
1. Diffuse pleural nodularity highly concerning for malignant pleural disease; mesothelioma vs pleural carcinomatosis.
2. Six biopsies obtained; expect adequate tissue for full IHC characterization and potential molecular analysis.
3. Talc pleurodesis performed; tube to remain until drainage <150 mL/24h, then clamp trial per nursing protocol.
4. Results to multidisciplinary thoracic oncology tumor board.
5. Oncology and thoracic surgery co-management; if mesothelioma confirmed, discuss HIPEC/P/D vs systemic therapy.
6. Follow-up IP clinic 2 weeks post-discharge.

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: table_with_tabs, missing_section

ICU BRONCHOSCOPY NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD   FELLOW: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 63M

INDICATION: Ventilated patient; bilateral opacities; R/O diffuse alveolar hemorrhage (DAH) vs pneumonia; ANCA-associated vasculitis (M31.31) on treatment.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: New bilateral infiltrates, mechanically ventilated patient; suspected DAH (D69.0 provisional)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; serial BAL consistent with DAH (progressively bloodier aliquots); awaiting pathology/cytology

PROCEDURE: Flexible bronchoscopy, BAL bilateral (CPT 31624)

ANESTHESIA: No additional sedation; patient on propofol 35 mcg/kg/min + fentanyl drip (baseline ICU). Paralytic held during procedure for cough assessment.

MONITORING: Continuous arterial line, SpO2, ETCO2, invasive BP.

PROCEDURE:

EBUS scope not used; standard Olympus BF-P290 via ETT (8.0 ETT). Airways inspected:

	Location	Mucosal Appearance	BAL Aliquot Results
	Trachea	Diffuse erythema; blood-tinged secretions	—
	Right upper lobe B1	Erythematous; no discrete lesion	Aliquot 1: pink-tinged
	Right lower lobe B10	Erythematous	Aliquot 2: bright red
	Left lower lobe B9	Diffuse erythema	Aliquots 1–3: progressively bloodier (serial DAH pattern confirmed)

Serial BAL protocol (LLL B9, primary site): 3 × 60 mL aliquots, each progressively bloodier — diagnostic for DAH.

BAL fluid analysis ordered:
  Hemosiderin-laden macrophages (Prussian Blue stain)
  Cytology and cell differential
  BAL Golde score (hemosiderin index)
  AFB, fungal, bacterial culture
  Urinalysis + anti-GBM, ANCA re-check (concurrent with BAL)

COMPLICATIONS: Transient SpO2 drop to 86% during RLL BAL; FiO2 increased to 1.0 for 3 minutes; recovery to 95% on FiO2 0.7. No pneumothorax. EBL: moderate (BAL return heavily blood-tinged but consistent with DAH, not iatrogenic).

PLAN:
1. DAH pattern confirmed bronchoscopically; pulse-dose methylprednisolone 1 g IV x3 days initiated.
2. Cyclophosphamide vs rituximab decision pending rheumatology.
3. Plasmapheresis consult for severe DAH with ANCA.
4. Repeat bronchoscopy in 48–72h if clinical worsening.

BRONCHOSCOPY PROCEDURE NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]    PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED]
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   AGE/SEX: 48-year-old 48-year-old female [sic]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]

Indication: Evaluation of recurrent right pleural effusion; pre-thoracoscopy airway assessment; known metastatic breast carcinoma.

Consent: Written informed consent obtained and documented in the electronic health record.

Technique: Flexible bronchoscopy performed with the patient in the semi-recumbent position under moderate sedation (midazolam 1 mg + fentanyl 50 mcg IV). Supplemental O2 via nasal cannula; SpO2 maintained >93%. Topical lidocaine 4% spray to oropharynx; 2 mL 2% lidocaine instilled at vocal cords via working channel.

Findings:
- Vocal cords: symmetric, normal abduction/adduction
- Trachea: patent, midline; no extrinsic compression
- Carina: sharp, midline; no widening suggesting subcarinal adenopathy
- Right mainstem bronchus: mild erythema of mucosa; no endobronchial lesion; patientpatient [sic] airways down to subsegmental level
- Left mainstem: unremarkable
- RLL posterior basal segment (B10): small whitish mucosal irregularity, <3 mm; endobronchial biopsy x2 performed
- No active bleeding post-biopsy

Specimens:
  Specimen 1: RLL B10 endobronchial biopsy (x2 forceps passes) — formalin, permanent H&E + ER/PR/HER2 IHC
  Specimen 2: BAL RLL (60 mL saline, 32 mL return) — cytology, culture

Plan:
1. Pathology result of RLL biopsy; if positive for endobronchial met, will change therapeutic approach.
2. Proceed with medical thoracoscopy for pleural effusion evaluation as planned (next available OR date).
3. Follow-up per IP clinic protocol.
Complications: None. Estimated blood loss: <1 mL.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOTE TEXT:

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, inconsistent_formatting, ambiguous_laterality

OPERATIVE NOTE — CRYOBIOPSY / NAVIGATIONAL BRONCHOSCOPY
PERFORMING PHYSICIAN: [NAME:REDACTED], MD, DAABIP
FELLOW (supervised): [NAME:REDACTED], MD
DATE: [REDACTED]    PATIENT: [PATIENT]    MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]    AGE/SEX: 61F

COMPLICATIONS (per resident pre-op note — listed first as template artifact):
Anticipated: pneumothorax risk ~10–15% with cryobiopsy; bleeding risk low.
Actual: [see end of note]

PLAN (pre-documented):
1. Post-procedure CXR.
2. Observe 2h in recovery; if no pneumothorax, discharge.
3. Pathology results in 5–7 days; follow-up lung nodule clinic.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Right middle lobe 1.6 cm solid nodule, PET-equivocal (SUVmax 2.1) (R91.1)
POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Same; biopsy obtained; pending pathology

PROCEDURE: Robotic navigational bronchoscopy with cryobiopsy (CPT 31627; 31628)

ANESTHESIA: General anesthesia; TIVA; size 7.5 ETT

CLINICAL CONTEXT: 61-year-old female, never-smoker, incidentally detected RML nodule (solid, 1.6 cm) on surveillance CT; PET mildly avid (SUV 2.1 vs background 1.4); growth on 6-month interval CT from 1.1 to 1.6 cm; referred for tissue diagnosis.

INSTRUMENTS: Ion Robotic Bronchoscope; 1.1 mm cryoprobe (Erbe); radial EBUS 20 MHz.

PROCEDURE:
Robotic bronchoscope introduced via ETT. Survey of bilateral airways: right [correction: confirmed RIGHT side — RML target throughout] upper, middle, lower airways inspected. No endobronchial lesion. Navigation plan to RML medial segment (B5). Catheter advanced under robotic guidance; shape-sensing confirmation within 3 mm. rEBUS deployed: concentric ring with central vessel artifact — consistent with within-lesion probe position. Fluoroscopic AP and lateral confirmed probe adjacent to RML opacity.

Cryoprobe introduced: 5 freeze-thaw cycles, 2.0 seconds each; adequate tissue cores retrieved (2–4 mm diameter each). Visual: tissue appeared fibrotic/solid. Bleeding post-biopsy: minimal, self-limited. Bronchial blocker pre-positioned in RML bronchus as precaution — released after hemostasis confirmed.

SPECIMENS:
  A: RML cryobiopsies x5 (2.0 sec cycles) — formalin; H&E + molecular hold
  B: RML brushings x2 — ThinPrep
  C: RML BAL 50 mL — culture, cytology

ACTUAL COMPLICATIONS: Post-procedure CXR: no pneumothorax. Mild hemoptysis (~5 mL, self-limited, resolved before discharge). EBL: ~5 mL.

FOLLOW-UP: Results to lung nodule program; oncology referral if malignant.

BEDSIDE PROCEDURE NOTE — PERCUTANEOUS DILATIONAL TRACHEOSTOMY
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD   PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 71F

Indication: Prolonged MV, ICU day 16; failure to wean due to severe ICU-acquired weakness; anticipated tracheostomy-dependent ventilation.
Anesthesia: Propofol 100 mg + fentanyl 75 mcg + succinylcholine 100 mg IV bolus; bedside ICU.
Procedure: Percutaneous dilational tracheostomy (PDT), Ciaglia Blue Rhino technique, ring 2–3 interspace, bronchoscopic guidance throughout. Shiley 6.0 cuffed XLT (extended-length) trach placed due to large neck habitus. Bronchoscopic confirmation: tube tip 5 cm above carina, midline, no posterior wall injury.
Findings: Tracheal entry confirmed between rings 2 and 3; blue-lit needle visible bronchoscopically; clean single-pass dilation; good hemostasis.
Complications: None. EBL: minimal.
Plan: Standard PDT care bundle; SLP consult; ventilator weaning protocol in AM.

T-TUBE INSERTION NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 61F

Indication: Recurrent post-radiation tracheal stenosis (adenoid cystic carcinoma, s/p resection and XRT); three prior balloon dilations with restenosis at 4–8 weeks; not candidate for re-resection.
Anesthesia: GA, TIVA, spontaneous ventilation via rigid bronchoscope.
Procedure: Rigid bronchoscopy; Nd:YAG laser (radial incisions); balloon dilation 14 mm; Montgomery T-tube 14 mm × 80 mm × 35 mm placed via rigid barrel. Sidearm exteriorized at ring 3 stoma (pre-existing from prior trach, epithelialized). Upper limb 1.5 cm above vocal cords. Flexible bronchoscopy confirmed lumen patent, distal end 3 cm above carina. patientpatient [sic] — patient tolerated procedure without hemodynamic instability.

NOTE TEXT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EDGE_CASE_TAGS: ultra_short, missing_section

EMERGENCY PROCEDURE NOTE
DATE: [REDACTED]   PROVIDER: [NAME:REDACTED], MD   PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE: 48M

EMERGENCY BRONCHOSCOPY — DISLODGED AIRWAY STENT WITH ACUTE OBSTRUCTION
Indication: Known tracheal Dumon stent (placed 8 weeks prior); acute stridor, dyspnea, SpO2 72% on arrival.
Procedure: Emergency rigid bronchoscopy; stent retrieval; balloon bronchoplasty; repeat stent placement.
Findings: Dumon stent migrated distally into LMB; near-complete obstruction of LMB orifice; RMB patent. Stent grasped with rigid forceps; extracted. Post-removal: LMB open. Balloon (12 mm) to LMB for residual stenosis. New Dumon stent placed in trachea (18 mm × 40 mm).
Complications: Hypoxic cardiac arrest (PEA) during induction — ACLS initiated; ROSC in 2 minutes; amiodarone 300 mg IV; rhythm: sinus tachycardia post-ROSC. Patient stabilized; airway secured. Admitted to ICU intubated. Cardiology consult placed.
Outcome: Patient extubated ICU day 2. Tracheal stent in position. Adverse event report filed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EDGE_CASE_TAGS: reordered_sections, noisy_units

PROCEDURE NOTE — T-TUBE COMPLICATED BY VALVE MIGRATION AND ACUTE OBSTRUCTION
DATE: [REDACTED]   ATTENDING: [NAME:REDACTED], MD
PATIENT: [PATIENT]   MRN: [MRN:REDACTED]   AGE/SEX: 55M

COMPLICATIONS DOCUMENTATION (per institutional reporting protocol, listed at top):
Complication type: Mucus impaction with acute obstruction of Montgomery T-tube; emergent bronchoscopic clearance required. Grade: Moderate (Clavien-Dindo III equivalent). Outcome: Resolved; patient discharged. No intubation required. Adverse event report filed.

PRE-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: Montgomery T-tube in situ (placed 4 months prior, post-intubation subglottic stenosis); acute dyspnea and stridor (J95.5; J98.01)

POST-PROCEDURE DIAGNOSIS: T-tube mucus impaction cleared; tube position intact and satisfactory

PROCEDURE: Flexible bronchoscopy through T-tube sidearm; mechanical clearance (CPT 31622)

ANESTHESIA: Moderate sedation (midazolam 2 mg, fentanyl 75 mcg IV); supplemental O2 via face mask.

Medication note: Patient had taken prescribed mucolytic (guaifenesin) 6 0 0mg [noisy — intended 600 mg] TID; compliance reported as poor over preceding week.

PROCEDURE:
Patient presented to IP clinic in moderate distress; SpO2 90% on room air; audible stridor. T-tube sidearm examined externally: sidearm plug in place (patient had not removed per instructions). Sidearm plug removed: immediate partial relief of symptoms. Flexible bronchoscope (4.9 mm) introduced via sidearm. Tracheal limb: near-complete occlusion with thick, tenacious dark green mucoid material and hardened mucoid cast extending from mid-tracheal limb to distal end. Upper limb (above cords) also partially occluded. Mechanical debridement: suction catheter + forceps piecemeal extraction; warm saline lavage (0.9% NaCl, 10 mL aliquots x6) instilled via sidearm. After 15 minutes of lavage and suction: T-tube lumen fully patent; SpO2 97%.

T-tube structural integrity: intact; no fracture; sidearm joint intact.

SPECIMENS: Mucus sent for culture.

POST-PROCEDURE PLAN:
1. Mucolytic compliance counseling; prescribe N-acetylcysteine nebulized 20% solution BID.
2. Saline nebulization QID mandatory.
3. T-tube inner surface hygiene: daily brushing protocol with pipe cleaner reinstructed.
4. IP clinic in 2 weeks; repeat flexible bronchoscopy via sidearm to confirm patency.
5. Pulmonary hygiene program referral.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
